content.text:
The backend container crashed on startup with NameError: name 'Numeric' is not defined in app/models/review.py when defining credibility_score.

Fix: Import Numeric from SQLAlchemy in review.py so the credibility_score column can use Numeric(4, 3) without error.
Alembic migration 002_extended_schema.py crashed with sqlalchemy.exc.ProgrammingError because the constraint cms_pages_slug_key did not exist when running ALTER TABLE cms_pages DROP CONSTRAINT cms_pages_slug_key.

Fix: In 002_extended_schema.py, replace op.drop_constraint('cms_pages_slug_key', 'cms_pages', type_='unique') with op.execute("ALTER TABLE cms_pages DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS cms_pages_slug_key") to make the migration idempotent.
Please apply these fixes using the approved AI tool and ensure the project passes CI.

1	import enum
2	from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
3	from sqlalchemy import (
4	    String, Text, DateTime, ForeignKey, Integer, Boolean,
5	    Enum as SAEnum, CheckConstraint, UniqueConstraint,
6	)
7	from sqlalchemy.orm import Mapped, mapped_column, relationship
8	from app.database import Base
9	
10	# Anti-spam: minimum gap between two reviews on the same order
11	REVIEW_RATE_LIMIT_MINUTES = 10
12	
13	# Follow-up window after initial review
14	FOLLOWUP_WINDOW_DAYS = 14
15	
16	# Max images per review
17	MAX_REVIEW_IMAGES = 6
18	
19	# Max comment length
20	MAX_COMMENT_LENGTH = 1000
21	
22	
23	class ReviewSubjectType(str, enum.Enum):
24	    product = "product"
25	    exam_type = "exam_type"
26	    catalog_item = "catalog_item"
27	
28	
29	class Review(Base):
30	    """
31	    Customer/patient review linked to a specific order.
32	
33	    Submission rules (enforced at application layer):
34	      - 1–5 star rating
35	      - comment ≤ 1,000 chars
36	      - max 6 images (via ReviewImage)
37	      - follow-up reviews allowed within 14 days of parent, one per order
38	      - anti-spam: 1 review per order per 10 minutes
39	
40	    Credibility score (computed and stored at submission time):
41	      - Verified order (is_completed=True): base weight 1.0
42	      - Follow-up edit: ×0.5 multiplier
43	      - Rapid repeat within 10 min on same order: −0.2 penalty
44	      - Clamped to [0.0, 1.0]
45	
46	    Moderation fields:
47	      - is_pinned: surfaces the review at the top of listings
48	      - is_collapsed: hides the review body from public listings (soft-hide)
49	      - store_id: allows per-store sorting / moderation scopes
50	    """
51	    __tablename__ = "reviews"
52	
53	    id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(primary_key=True, index=True)
54	
55	    # Every review must be tied to an order
56	    order_id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(
57	        ForeignKey("orders.id"), nullable=False, index=True
58	    )
59	    reviewer_id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(
60	        ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False, index=True
61	    )
62	
63	    # What is being reviewed
64	    subject_type: Mapped[ReviewSubjectType] = mapped_column(
65	        SAEnum(ReviewSubjectType), nullable=False
66	    )
67	    subject_id: Mapped[int | None] = mapped_column(Integer, nullable=True)
68	    subject_text: Mapped[str | None] = mapped_column(String(255), nullable=True)
69	
70	    # Review content
71	    rating: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(Integer, nullable=False)
72	    comment: Mapped[str | None] = mapped_column(String(MAX_COMMENT_LENGTH), nullable=True)
73	    tags: Mapped[str | None] = mapped_column(Text, nullable=True)   # JSON array stored as text
74	
75	    # Follow-up support
76	    is_followup: Mapped[bool] = mapped_column(Boolean, default=False, nullable=False)
77	    parent_review_id: Mapped[int | None] = mapped_column(
78	        ForeignKey("reviews.id"), nullable=True
79	    )
80	    followup_deadline: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(
81	        DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True
82	    )  # parent.created_at + 14 days; NULL on initial reviews
83	
84	    # Anti-spam tracking
85	    submitted_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(
86	        DateTime(timezone=True),
87	        default=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc),
88	        nullable=False,
89	    )
90	
91	    # Credibility score — persisted so listing sorts don't need recomputation
92	    credibility_score: Mapped[float] = mapped_column(
93	        Numeric(4, 3), nullable=False, default=0.0
94	    )
95	
96	    # Moderation
97	    is_pinned: Mapped[bool] = mapped_column(Boolean, default=False, nullable=False, index=True)
98	    is_collapsed: Mapped[bool] = mapped_column(Boolean, default=False, nullable=False)
99	    store_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(100), nullable=False, default="default", index=True)
100	    moderation_note: Mapped[str | None] = mapped_column(String(500), nullable=True)
101	    moderated_by: Mapped[int | None] = mapped_column(ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=True)
102	    moderated_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True)
103	
104	    created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(
105	        DateTime(timezone=True),
106	        default=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc),
107	        nullable=False,
108	    )
109	
110	    order = relationship("Order", back_populates="reviews")
111	    reviewer = relationship("User", foreign_keys=[reviewer_id])
112	    moderator = relationship("User", foreign_keys=[moderated_by])
113	    parent_review = relationship("Review", remote_side="Review.id", foreign_keys=[parent_review_id])
114	    followup_reviews = relationship(
115	        "Review",
116	        foreign_keys="[Review.parent_review_id]",
117	        back_populates="parent_review",
118	    )
119	    images = relationship(
120	        "ReviewImage",
121	        back_populates="review",
122	        cascade="all, delete-orphan",
123	    )
124	
125	    __table_args__ = (
126	        CheckConstraint("rating >= 1 AND rating <= 5", name="ck_reviews_rating_range"),
127	        CheckConstraint(
128	            "credibility_score >= 0.0 AND credibility_score <= 1.0",
129	            name="ck_reviews_credibility_range",
130	        ),
131	    )
132	
133	
134	class ReviewImage(Base):
135	    """
136	    Image attachment for a review. Maximum 6 per review (enforced at app layer).
137	    Stores original filename, MIME type, size, and SHA-256 fingerprint.
138	    """
139	    __tablename__ = "review_images"
140	
141	    id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(primary_key=True, index=True)
142	    review_id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(
143	        ForeignKey("reviews.id", ondelete="CASCADE"), nullable=False, index=True
144	    )
145	
146	    original_filename: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(255), nullable=False)
147	    stored_filename: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(500), nullable=False)   # UUID-based on disk
148	    mime_type: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(100), nullable=False)         # image/jpeg, image/png…
149	    file_size: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(Integer, nullable=False)             # bytes
150	    sha256_fingerprint: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(64), nullable=False) # hex digest
151	
152	    created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(
153	        DateTime(timezone=True),
154	        default=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc),
155	        nullable=False,
156	    )
157	
158	    review = relationship("Review", back_populates="images")
159	
160	    __table_args__ = (
161	        CheckConstraint("file_size > 0", name="ck_review_images_size_positive"),
162	    )
163

1	"""Extended schema: exam items, packages, orders, notifications, CMS revisions,
2	   review images, catalog attachments, user session fields.
3	
4	Revision ID: 002_extended
5	Revises: 001_initial
6	Create Date: 2024-01-02 00:00:00.000000
7	"""
8	from typing import Sequence, Union
9	from alembic import op
10	import sqlalchemy as sa
11	
12	revision: str = "002_extended"
13	down_revision: Union[str, None] = "001_initial"
14	branch_labels: Union[str, Sequence[str], None] = None
15	depends_on: Union[str, Sequence[str], None] = None
16	
17	
18	def upgrade() -> None:
19	
20	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
21	    # 1. Extend users table
22	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
23	    op.add_column("users", sa.Column("last_login", sa.DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True))
24	    op.add_column("users", sa.Column("session_token_hash", sa.String(255), nullable=True))
25	    op.add_column("users", sa.Column("session_expires_at", sa.DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True))
26	    op.add_column("users", sa.Column("failed_login_attempts", sa.Integer, nullable=False, server_default="0"))
27	    op.add_column("users", sa.Column("locked_until", sa.DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True))
28	    op.add_column("users", sa.Column("updated_at", sa.DateTime(timezone=True),
29	                                     nullable=False, server_default=sa.func.now()))
30	
31	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
32	    # 2. Exam items dictionary
33	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
34	    op.create_table(
35	        "exam_items",
36	        sa.Column("id", sa.Integer, primary_key=True, index=True),
37	        sa.Column("code", sa.String(50), unique=True, nullable=False, index=True),
38	        sa.Column("name", sa.String(255), nullable=False),
39	        sa.Column("description", sa.Text, nullable=True),
40	        sa.Column("unit", sa.String(50), nullable=True),
41	        sa.Column("ref_range_min", sa.Numeric(12, 4), nullable=True),
42	        sa.Column("ref_range_max", sa.Numeric(12, 4), nullable=True),
43	        sa.Column("ref_range_text", sa.String(255), nullable=True),
44	        sa.Column(
45	            "applicable_sex",
46	            sa.Enum("male", "female", "all", name="examitemsex"),
47	            nullable=False,
48	            server_default="all",
49	        ),
50	        sa.Column("min_age_years", sa.Integer, nullable=True),
51	        sa.Column("max_age_years", sa.Integer, nullable=True),
52	        sa.Column("contraindications", sa.Text, nullable=True),
53	        sa.Column("collection_method", sa.String(255), nullable=True),
54	        sa.Column("preparation_instructions", sa.Text, nullable=True),
55	        sa.Column("is_active", sa.Boolean, nullable=False, server_default="true"),
56	        sa.Column("created_by", sa.Integer, sa.ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False),
57	        sa.Column("created_at", sa.DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=False, server_default=sa.func.now()),
58	        sa.Column("updated_at", sa.DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=False, server_default=sa.func.now()),
59	        sa.CheckConstraint(
60	            "(ref_range_min IS NULL OR ref_range_max IS NULL) OR ref_range_min <= ref_range_max",
61	            name="ck_exam_items_ref_range_order",
62	        ),
63	        sa.CheckConstraint(
64	            "(min_age_years IS NULL OR max_age_years IS NULL) OR min_age_years <= max_age_years",
65	            name="ck_exam_items_age_range_order",
66	        ),
67	    )
68	
69	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
70	    # 3. Packages (versioned bundles)
71	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
72	    op.create_table(
73	        "packages",
74	        sa.Column("id", sa.Integer, primary_key=True, index=True),
75	        sa.Column("name", sa.String(255), nullable=False),
76	        sa.Column("description", sa.Text, nullable=True),
77	        sa.Column("version", sa.Integer, nullable=False, server_default="1"),
78	        sa.Column("price", sa.Numeric(10, 2), nullable=False, server_default="0"),
79	        sa.Column("is_active", sa.Boolean, nullable=False, server_default="true"),
80	        sa.Column("created_by", sa.Integer, sa.ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False),
81	        sa.Column("created_at", sa.DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=False, server_default=sa.func.now()),
82	        sa.UniqueConstraint("name", "version", name="uq_packages_name_version"),
83	    )
84	
85	    op.create_table(
86	        "package_items",
87	        sa.Column("id", sa.Integer, primary_key=True, index=True),
88	        sa.Column("package_id", sa.Integer, sa.ForeignKey("packages.id"), nullable=False),
89	        sa.Column("exam_item_id", sa.Integer, sa.ForeignKey("exam_items.id"), nullable=False),
90	        sa.Column("is_required", sa.Boolean, nullable=False, server_default="true"),
91	        sa.Column("item_code_snapshot", sa.String(50), nullable=False),
92	        sa.Column("item_name_snapshot", sa.String(255), nullable=False),
93	        sa.Column("unit_snapshot", sa.String(50), nullable=True),
94	        sa.Column("ref_range_snapshot", sa.String(255), nullable=True),
95	        sa.Column("collection_method_snapshot", sa.String(255), nullable=True),
96	        sa.Column("created_at", sa.DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=False, server_default=sa.func.now()),
97	        sa.UniqueConstraint("package_id", "exam_item_id", name="uq_package_items_pkg_item"),
98	    )
99	
100	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
101	    # 4. Add package_id to exams
102	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
103	    op.add_column("exams", sa.Column("package_id", sa.Integer,
104	                                     sa.ForeignKey("packages.id"), nullable=True))
105	
106	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
107	    # 5. Extend catalog_items with agricultural fields
108	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
109	    op.add_column("catalog_items", sa.Column("grade", sa.String(50), nullable=True))
110	    op.add_column("catalog_items", sa.Column("specifications", sa.Text, nullable=True))
111	    op.add_column("catalog_items", sa.Column("origin", sa.String(255), nullable=True))
112	    op.add_column("catalog_items", sa.Column("harvest_batch", sa.String(100), nullable=True))
113	    op.add_column("catalog_items", sa.Column("harvest_date", sa.DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True))
114	    op.add_column("catalog_items", sa.Column("packaging_info", sa.String(500), nullable=True))
115	    op.add_column("catalog_items", sa.Column("shelf_life_days", sa.Integer, nullable=True))
116	    op.create_check_constraint("ck_catalog_items_stock_non_negative", "catalog_items", "stock_quantity >= 0")
117	    op.create_check_constraint("ck_catalog_items_price_non_negative", "catalog_items", "price >= 0")
118	    op.create_check_constraint(
119	        "ck_catalog_items_shelf_life_positive", "catalog_items",
120	        "shelf_life_days IS NULL OR shelf_life_days > 0",
121	    )
122	
123	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
124	    # 6. Catalog attachments
125	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
126	    op.create_table(
127	        "catalog_attachments",
128	        sa.Column("id", sa.Integer, primary_key=True, index=True),
129	        sa.Column("catalog_item_id", sa.Integer,
130	                  sa.ForeignKey("catalog_items.id", ondelete="CASCADE"),
131	                  nullable=False, index=True),
132	        sa.Column("original_filename", sa.String(255), nullable=False),
133	        sa.Column("stored_filename", sa.String(500), nullable=False),
134	        sa.Column("mime_type", sa.String(100), nullable=False),
135	        sa.Column("file_size", sa.Integer, nullable=False),
136	        sa.Column("sha256_fingerprint", sa.String(64), nullable=False),
137	        sa.Column("uploaded_by", sa.Integer, sa.ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False),
138	        sa.Column("created_at", sa.DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=False, server_default=sa.func.now()),
139	        sa.CheckConstraint("file_size <= 5242880", name="ck_catalog_attachments_size_limit"),
140	        sa.CheckConstraint("file_size > 0", name="ck_catalog_attachments_size_positive"),
141	    )
142	
143	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
144	    # 7. Orders + order items
145	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
146	    op.create_table(
147	        "orders",
148	        sa.Column("id", sa.Integer, primary_key=True, index=True),
149	        sa.Column("order_number", sa.String(50), unique=True, nullable=False, index=True),
150	        sa.Column("customer_id", sa.Integer, sa.ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False, index=True),
151	        sa.Column(
152	            "order_type",
153	            sa.Enum("exam", "product", name="ordertype"),
154	            nullable=False,
155	        ),
156	        sa.Column(
157	            "status",
158	            sa.Enum("pending", "confirmed", "in_progress", "completed", "cancelled", name="orderstatus"),
159	            nullable=False,
160	            server_default="pending",
161	        ),
162	        sa.Column("is_completed", sa.Boolean, nullable=False, server_default="false"),
163	        sa.Column("total_amount", sa.Numeric(12, 2), nullable=False, server_default="0"),
164	        sa.Column("notes", sa.Text, nullable=True),
165	        sa.Column("created_at", sa.DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=False, server_default=sa.func.now()),
166	        sa.Column("updated_at", sa.DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=False, server_default=sa.func.now()),
167	        sa.CheckConstraint("total_amount >= 0", name="ck_orders_total_non_negative"),
168	    )
169	
170	    op.create_table(
171	        "order_items",
172	        sa.Column("id", sa.Integer, primary_key=True, index=True),
173	        sa.Column("order_id", sa.Integer,
174	                  sa.ForeignKey("orders.id", ondelete="CASCADE"),
175	                  nullable=False, index=True),
176	        sa.Column("item_type", sa.String(50), nullable=False),
177	        sa.Column("item_id", sa.Integer, nullable=False),
178	        sa.Column("item_name_snapshot", sa.String(255), nullable=False),
179	        sa.Column("item_sku_snapshot", sa.String(100), nullable=True),
180	        sa.Column("unit_price_snapshot", sa.Numeric(10, 2), nullable=False),
181	        sa.Column("quantity", sa.Integer, nullable=False, server_default="1"),
182	        sa.Column("subtotal", sa.Numeric(12, 2), nullable=False),
183	        sa.Column("item_data_snapshot", sa.Text, nullable=True),
184	        sa.Column("created_at", sa.DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=False, server_default=sa.func.now()),
185	        sa.CheckConstraint("quantity > 0", name="ck_order_items_quantity_positive"),
186	        sa.CheckConstraint("unit_price_snapshot >= 0", name="ck_order_items_price_non_negative"),
187	        sa.CheckConstraint("subtotal >= 0", name="ck_order_items_subtotal_non_negative"),
188	    )
189	
190	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
191	    # 8. Rebuild reviews: add order_id, images, tags, follow-up, anti-spam
192	    #    Drop the old enum and recreate with "catalog_item" added.
193	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
194	    # Add new columns to existing reviews table
195	    op.add_column("reviews", sa.Column("order_id", sa.Integer,
196	                                        sa.ForeignKey("orders.id"), nullable=True))  # nullable during migration
197	    op.add_column("reviews", sa.Column("tags", sa.Text, nullable=True))
198	    op.add_column("reviews", sa.Column("is_followup", sa.Boolean, nullable=False, server_default="false"))
199	    op.add_column("reviews", sa.Column("parent_review_id", sa.Integer,
200	                                        sa.ForeignKey("reviews.id"), nullable=True))
201	    op.add_column("reviews", sa.Column("followup_deadline", sa.DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True))
202	    op.add_column("reviews", sa.Column("submitted_at", sa.DateTime(timezone=True),
203	                                        nullable=False, server_default=sa.func.now()))
204	
205	    # Extend the enum type (PostgreSQL supports ALTER TYPE … ADD VALUE)
206	    op.execute("ALTER TYPE reviewsubjecttype ADD VALUE IF NOT EXISTS 'catalog_item'")
207	
208	    # Add rating constraint
209	    op.create_check_constraint("ck_reviews_rating_range", "reviews", "rating >= 1 AND rating <= 5")
210	
211	    # Review images
212	    op.create_table(
213	        "review_images",
214	        sa.Column("id", sa.Integer, primary_key=True, index=True),
215	        sa.Column("review_id", sa.Integer,
216	                  sa.ForeignKey("reviews.id", ondelete="CASCADE"),
217	                  nullable=False, index=True),
218	        sa.Column("original_filename", sa.String(255), nullable=False),
219	        sa.Column("stored_filename", sa.String(500), nullable=False),
220	        sa.Column("mime_type", sa.String(100), nullable=False),
221	        sa.Column("file_size", sa.Integer, nullable=False),
222	        sa.Column("sha256_fingerprint", sa.String(64), nullable=False),
223	        sa.Column("created_at", sa.DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=False, server_default=sa.func.now()),
224	        sa.CheckConstraint("file_size > 0", name="ck_review_images_size_positive"),
225	    )
226	
227	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
228	    # 9. Notifications
229	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
230	    op.create_table(
231	        "notifications",
232	        sa.Column("id", sa.Integer, primary_key=True, index=True),
233	        sa.Column("recipient_id", sa.Integer, sa.ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False, index=True),
234	        sa.Column(
235	            "notification_type",
236	            sa.Enum("info", "warning", "error", "success", "system", name="notificationtype"),
237	            nullable=False,
238	            server_default="info",
239	        ),
240	        sa.Column("title", sa.String(255), nullable=False),
241	        sa.Column("body", sa.Text, nullable=False),
242	        sa.Column("related_entity_type", sa.String(50), nullable=True),
243	        sa.Column("related_entity_id", sa.Integer, nullable=True),
244	        sa.Column("is_read", sa.Boolean, nullable=False, server_default="false"),
245	        sa.Column("read_at", sa.DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True),
246	        sa.Column(
247	            "status",
248	            sa.Enum("pending", "delivered", "retrying", "failed", name="notificationstatus"),
249	            nullable=False,
250	            server_default="pending",
251	        ),
252	        sa.Column("delivery_attempts", sa.Integer, nullable=False, server_default="0"),
253	        sa.Column("last_attempted_at", sa.DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True),
254	        sa.Column("next_retry_at", sa.DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True, index=True),
255	        sa.Column("delivered_at", sa.DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True),
256	        sa.Column("failure_reason", sa.Text, nullable=True),
257	        sa.Column("created_at", sa.DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=False, server_default=sa.func.now()),
258	        sa.CheckConstraint("delivery_attempts <= 4", name="ck_notifications_max_attempts"),
259	        sa.CheckConstraint("delivery_attempts >= 0", name="ck_notifications_attempts_non_negative"),
260	    )
261	
262	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
263	    # 10. Rebuild cms_pages: add status, multi-store, SEO, sitemap, revisions
264	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
265	    # Create new enum types for CMS
266	    op.execute(
267	        "CREATE TYPE cmspagestatustype AS ENUM ('draft', 'review', 'published', 'archived')"
268	    )

You are a senior full-stack remediation engineer.
Goal:

====================================================
MANDATORY FIX SCOPE (must be fully addressed)
====================================================
1) Fix message thread API contract mismatch (Blocker)
- Frontend currently calls `/threads...` while backend serves under `/messages/threads...`.
- Make message center fully functional by aligning contract consistently.
- Update frontend calls, route integrations, and tests accordingly.
- Ensure all thread operations work with aligned endpoints:
  - list threads
  - get thread detail
  - send thread message
  - mark thread read
  - archive thread
  - resolve alias
2) Fix notifications contract mismatch (Blocker)
- Align frontend/store + tests with backend notification routes and response fields.
- Fix all of these mismatches:
  - unread count key should match backend (`unread_count`)
  - mark-all-read route
  - preferences routes (`/preferences/me`)
  - metrics route (`/admin/metrics`)
- Ensure UI tabs/filters use valid notification_type values defined by backend enums.
3) Remove insecure direct static file exposure (High)
- `/uploads` static mount currently bypasses protected download/integrity/audit endpoints.
- Remove or hard-restrict public file serving path so protected files are only accessible via authenticated endpoints.
- Preserve existing authenticated download behavior for catalog/review files.
- Add regression tests covering unauthorized direct file access attempts.
4) Fix frontend review subject taxonomy mismatch (High)
- Frontend uses invalid subject types vs backend enum.
- Align frontend create/filter options to backend schema (or migrate backend + all usages consistently if you choose that path).
- Ensure create/followup/filter flows use valid values and pass schema validation.
- Update tests.
5) Fix package diff UI contract mismatch (High)
- Frontend expects fields like `before/after/changed_fields`; backend returns different schema (`from_value/to_value`, `changes`).
- Update frontend mapping/rendering to match backend response contract.
- Ensure “what changed” workflow is accurate and robust.
6) Implement Prompt-required catalog filtering/search completeness (High)
- Add missing multi-criteria catalog capabilities required by prompt in a credible way:
  - status
  - tags
  - date range
  - priority
  - full-text search across title/name + description
- Wire frontend controls and backend query support (models/schemas/router/tests) so this is not superficial UI-only behavior.
- Keep offline-only constraints.
7) Repair stale/invalid API tests so coverage is trustworthy (High)
- Rewrite API tests that currently target obsolete endpoints/payloads.
- Ensure tests reflect actual implemented contracts after your fixes.
- Prioritize:
  - packages/versioning/diff tests
  - catalog payload/schema tests
  - notifications tests
  - thread/messages tests
- Add/strengthen security tests for:
  - object-level authorization
  - admin external API key boundaries
  - file-access protection
8) Fix broken admin nav path (Medium but required)
- Navbar uses wrong package setup route.
- Correct route link and add test.
9) Prompt compliance gap: review image type policy (Medium)
- Prompt says review images PNG/JPG.
- Enforce exactly PNG/JPG in backend validation + frontend file input accept list + tests.
10) Sensitive contact field encryption at rest (Medium, required for prompt fit)
- Prompt requires sensitive contact data encrypted at rest.
- Implement a clear, maintainable approach for user contact info (e.g., email/contact fields), including:
  - model/storage changes
  - read/write handling
  - masking/redaction consistency in logs/responses where appropriate
  - migration compatibility
  - tests
====================================================
ENGINEERING + QUALITY REQUIREMENTS
====================================================
- Keep architecture modular; avoid dumping logic into single files.
- Preserve offline-only behavior (no cloud dependencies).
- Do not remove existing security controls unless replaced with stronger equivalent.
- Keep RBAC/object-level checks explicit.
- Keep error handling user-friendly and API-consistent.
- Update README/docs where behavior or endpoints changed.
====================================================
TESTING REQUIREMENTS
====================================================
- Update and run relevant test suites.
- Add tests for all newly fixed high-risk paths.
- Ensure no stale endpoint assumptions remain.
- At minimum include:
  - authentication/authorization tests
  - contract tests for fixed routes and payloads
  - object-level authorization checks
  - file protection/security tests
  - frontend tests for fixed router/API usage and key interaction states
====================================================
DELIVERABLE FORMAT

## 1. BACKEND STRUCTURE

### Main Application Entry Point (`/c/Users/user/Desktop/eaglepoint_new/Week-2/TASK-60/repo/backend/app/main.py`)

```python
# All routers mounted with /api prefix
app.include_router(messages.router,      prefix=_API)
app.include_router(notifications.router, prefix=_API)
app.include_router(reviews.router,       prefix=_API)
app.include_router(catalog.router,       prefix=_API)
app.include_router(packages.router,      prefix=_API)

# Static file mount for uploads
app.mount(
    "/uploads",
    StaticFiles(directory=str(settings.attachments_path.parent)),
    name="uploads",
)
```

### Messages & Threads Endpoints

**Router prefix:** `/api/messages`

**Direct messages:**
- `POST /messages` — send encrypted message
- `GET /messages/inbox` — list received messages
- `GET /messages/sent` — list sent messages
- `GET /messages/inbox/unread-count` — badge counter
- `GET /messages/{message_id}` — fetch & decrypt body
- `PATCH /messages/{message_id}/read` — mark read
- `DELETE /messages/{message_id}`

**Conversation threads:**
- `POST /messages/threads` — create thread with participants, optional virtual IDs
- `GET /messages/threads` — list user's threads (brief summaries)
- `GET /messages/threads/{thread_id}` — get full thread with all messages
- `POST /messages/threads/{thread_id}/messages` — post message to thread
- `PATCH /messages/threads/{thread_id}/read` — mark thread read
- `PATCH /messages/threads/{thread_id}/archive` — archive thread

**Virtual contact relay (admin only):**
- `GET /messages/threads/{thread_id}/my-alias` — return user's virtual alias
- `GET /messages/threads/{thread_id}/resolve-alias/{alias}` — resolve alias to real user_id (admin only)

**Models:**
- `Message` — direct encrypted message
- `Thread` — conversation thread (optional `order_id` anchor)
- `ThreadParticipant` — membership with `virtual_contact_id` alias
- `ThreadMessage` — individual message in thread

**Key Fields:**
- `Message.body_encrypted` — Fernet-encrypted
- `Thread.use_virtual_ids` — boolean flag for masking
- `ThreadParticipant.virtual_contact_id` — e.g., "USR-A3F9"
- `ThreadMessage.is_system_message` — for order status updates

### Notifications Endpoints

**Router prefix:** `/api/notifications`

- `POST /notifications` — create notification (admin only)
- `POST /notifications/order-status` — emit order status event (admin, clinic_staff, catalog_manager)
  - `event_subtype` must be: `"accepted" | "arrived" | "completed" | "exception"`
- `GET /notifications` — list own notifications
- `GET /notifications/unread-count` — badge data
- `GET /notifications/{notif_id}`
- `PATCH /notifications/{notif_id}/read`
- `POST /notifications/mark-read` — bulk mark read
- `POST /notifications/mark-all-read`
- `DELETE /notifications/{notif_id}`
- `GET /notifications/preferences/me` — get user's preferences
- `PUT /notifications/preferences/me` — update preferences
- `GET /notifications/admin/metrics` — delivery metrics (admin only)

**Models:**
- `Notification` — with `NotificationType` enum (info, warning, error, success, system)
- `NotificationStatus` enum: pending, delivered, retrying, failed
- `UserNotificationPreference` — per-user opt-in flags for each category

**Response Fields:**
- `id, recipient_id, notification_type, event_subtype`
- `title, body`
- `related_entity_type, related_entity_id` (e.g., "order" → 123, "thread" → 45)
- `is_read, read_at`
- `status, delivery_attempts, next_retry_at, delivered_at, failure_reason`
- `created_at`

**Notification Preferences:**
- `notify_order_accepted, notify_order_arrived, notify_order_completed, notify_order_exception` (order-status events)
- `notify_new_message, notify_thread_reply` (messaging)
- `notify_system, notify_info` (general)

### Reviews Endpoints

**Router prefix:** `/api/reviews`

- `POST /reviews` — submit review
  - `subject_type` enum: `"product" | "exam_type" | "catalog_item"`
  - For `exam_type`: `subject_text` required (e.g., "Blood Test A")
  - For `product` / `catalog_item`: `subject_id` required
- `GET /reviews` — list reviews with filters
- `GET /reviews/summary` — aggregate stats
- `GET /reviews/{review_id}`
- `POST /reviews/{review_id}/followup` — follow-up review
- `POST /reviews/{review_id}/images` — upload image (max 6 per review, 5 MB each)
- `PATCH /reviews/{review_id}/pin` — pin review (admin/catalog_manager)
- `PATCH /reviews/{review_id}/unpin`
- `PATCH /reviews/{review_id}/collapse` — soft-hide (admin/catalog_manager)
- `PATCH /reviews/{review_id}/uncollapse`
- `DELETE /reviews/{review_id}` — hard delete (admin only)
- `GET /reviews/{review_id}/images/{image_id}` — download image

**Models:**
- `ReviewSubjectType` enum: `"product" | "exam_type" | "catalog_item"`
- `Review` — with `credibility_score` (Numeric(4,3), range 0.0-1.0)
- `ReviewImage` — with SHA-256 fingerprint

**Key Fields:**
- `Review.credibility_score` — computed from: order completion status, follow-up weight, rapid-repeat penalty
- `Review.is_pinned, is_collapsed, store_id` — moderation fields
- `Review.rating` — 1-5 stars
- `Review.comment` — max 1000 chars
- `Review.tags` — JSON array stored as text
- Anti-spam: 1 review per order per 10 minutes
- Follow-up window: 14 days after parent review

### Catalog Endpoints

**Router prefix:** `/api/catalog`

- `POST /catalog` — create item (admin, catalog_manager)
- `GET /catalog` — list with multi-criteria filtering
  - `search` — OR across name, description, category, origin
  - `name, category, grade, origin, harvest_batch` — exact match
  - `price_min, price_max, stock_min, stock_max, shelf_life_min, shelf_life_max` — ranges
  - `harvest_date_from, harvest_date_to` — date range
  - `active_only, in_stock` — boolean flags
  - `sort_by` enum: name, price, stock_quantity, shelf_life_days, created_at, updated_at, harvest_date
  - `sort_dir` enum: asc, desc
- `GET /catalog/{item_id}`
- `PUT /catalog/{item_id}` — update (admin, catalog_manager)
- `DELETE /catalog/{item_id}`
- `POST /catalog/{item_id}/attachments` — upload file
  - Allowed MIME types: `image/jpeg, image/png, image/webp, image/gif, application/pdf, application/vnd.ms-excel, application/msword, text/plain, text/csv`
  - Max 5 MB per file
- `GET /catalog/{item_id}/attachments` — list
- `GET /catalog/{item_id}/attachments/{attachment_id}` — download
- `DELETE /catalog/{item_id}/attachments/{attachment_id}`
- `GET /catalog/{item_id}/attachments/{attachment_id}/verify` — verify integrity (SHA-256)

**Models:**
- `CatalogItem` — agricultural product with grade, origin, harvest batch, packaging, shelf life
- `CatalogAttachment` — with SHA-256 fingerprint

**Key Fields:**
- `CatalogItem.grade` — quality level
- `CatalogItem.specifications` — JSON object (agronomic specs)
- `CatalogItem.harvest_batch` — lot/batch number
- `CatalogItem.shelf_life_days` — from harvest
- `CatalogAttachment.sha256_fingerprint` — for integrity

### Packages Endpoints

**Router prefix:** `/api/packages`

- `POST /packages` — create version 1
  - `items` array with `exam_item_id` and `is_required` flag
- `GET /packages` — list packages
  - `active_only` — filter for is_active=True
  - `name` — filter by name
- `GET /packages/{package_id}` — get full package with items
- `GET /packages/{package_id}/versions` — list all versions of same package
- `POST /packages/{package_id}/new-version` — bump version
  - Carries over fields not supplied in payload
  - Enforces at least one change
- `GET /packages/{package_id}/diff/{other_id}` — compare two versions
  - Returns metadata changes (name, price, validity_window_days)
  - Item-level: added, removed, changed (with field-by-field diffs)
- `PATCH /packages/{package_id}/activate` — set is_active=True, deactivate other versions
- `PATCH /packages/{package_id}/deactivate`
- `DELETE /packages/{package_id}`

**Models:**
- `Package` — with version number, immutable history
- `PackageItem` — denormalized snapshot of ExamItem fields

**Package Diff Response:**
```
{
  "from_version": 1,
  "to_version": 2,
  "metadata_changes": [
    {"field": "price", "from_value": 99.99, "to_value": 149.99},
    {"field": "validity_window_days", "from_value": 30, "to_value": 60}
  ],
  "items_added": [
    {"exam_item_id": 5, "code": "BUN", "name": "Blood Urea Nitrogen"}
  ],
  "items_removed": [...],
  "items_changed": [
    {
      "exam_item_id": 3,
      "code": "ALB",
      "name": "Albumin",
      "changes": {
        "is_required": {"from": false, "to": true}
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

### API Client (`/c/Users/user/Desktop/eaglepoint_new/Week-2/TASK-60/repo/frontend/src/api/index.js`)

```javascript
const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: '/api',
  headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
})

// Request interceptor: attach Bearer token from localStorage
api.interceptors.request.use(config => {
  const token = localStorage.getItem('access_token')
  if (token) {
    config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`
  }
  return config
})

// Response interceptor: auto-refresh on 401
// Uses refresh_token from localStorage to get new access_token
```

### Stores (Pinia)

#### `notifications.js`

```javascript
const store = {
  notifications: [],      // Array of notification objects
  unreadCount: 0,
  preferences: null,      // UserNotificationPreference object
  
  unread: computed(() => notifications.filter(n => !n.is_read))
}

// Methods:
fetchUnreadCount()        // GET /notifications/unread-count
fetchNotifications(params) // GET /notifications?params
markRead(id)              // PATCH /notifications/{id}/read
markAllRead()             // POST /notifications/mark-all-read
deleteNotification(id)    // DELETE /notifications/{id}
fetchPreferences()        // GET /notifications/preferences/me
savePreferences(prefs)    // PUT /notifications/preferences/me
startPolling(intervalMs)  // poll every 30s by default
```

**Field mapping from API response:**
- `n.is_read` — boolean
- `n.notification_type` — enum string (info, warning, error, success, system)
- `n.event_subtype` — order status string (accepted, arrived, completed, exception) or null
- `n.title, n.body` — display text
- `n.status` — delivery status (pending, delivered, retrying, failed)
- `n.related_entity_type, n.related_entity_id` — link to order, thread, etc.
- `n.created_at` — ISO timestamp

#### `auth.js`

```javascript
const store = {
  user: null,
  isAuthenticated: false,
  userRole: null,  // user?.role value
}

// Role check helper:
isRole(...roles) // check if userRole is in the list
```

## 4. DOCKER COMPOSE

**File:** `/c/Users/user/Desktop/eaglepoint_new/Week-2/TASK-60/repo/docker-compose.yml`

```yaml
services:
  db:
    image: postgres:16
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: tracecare
      POSTGRES_USER: tracecare
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: tracecare
    ports: 5432:5432
    
  backend:
    build: ./backend
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: postgresql://tracecare:tracecare@db:5432/tracecare
      SECRET_KEY: [32-char key]
      ENCRYPTION_KEY: [base64 Fernet key]
    ports: 8000:8000
    depends_on: db
    volumes: ./backend:/app
    command: python init_db.py && uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000
    
  frontend:
    build: ./frontend
    ports: 3000:80
    depends_on: backend
    
  tester:
    build: ./backend
    depends_on: backend
    volumes: 
      - ./backend:/app
      - ./API_tests:/API_tests
    command: pytest /API_tests -v
```

**Upload handling:**
- Backend mounts `/uploads` endpoint serving from `settings.attachments_path`
- Static files served directly by FastAPI (no CDN)
- All attachments stored on local filesystem

## 5. KEY ARCHITECTURAL PATTERNS & FIELD MAPPINGS

### Messages/Threads

| Aspect | Details |
|--------|---------|
| **Endpoint Prefix** | `/api/messages` |
| **Direct message URL** | `POST /messages` to send, `GET /messages/inbox` to receive |
| **Thread URL** | `POST /messages/threads` to create, `GET /messages/threads/{id}` to fetch |
| **Thread message URL** | `POST /messages/threads/{thread_id}/messages` |
| **Message encryption** | Fernet cipher; `body_encrypted` stored, `body` returned decrypted in responses |
| **Virtual contact IDs** | Only if `use_virtual_ids=True` at thread creation; format: "USR-A3F9" |
| **Unread tracking** | `ThreadParticipant.unread_count` incremented on message, reset on read |

### Notifications

| Aspect | Details |
|--------|---------|
| **Endpoint Prefix** | `/api/notifications` |
| **List call** | `GET /notifications?unread_only=true&notification_type=info` |
| **Unread badge** | `GET /notifications/unread-count` returns `{unread_count: N}` |
| **Mark read** | `PATCH /notifications/{id}/read` or `POST /mark-all-read` |
| **Response fields** | id, recipient_id, notification_type, event_subtype, title, body, is_read, status, related_entity_type, related_entity_id, created_at |
| **Preferences** | `GET/PUT /notifications/preferences/me` with fields: notify_order_accepted, notify_new_message, etc. |
| **Order-status subtypes** | "accepted", "arrived", "completed", "exception" |

### Reviews

| Aspect | Details |
|--------|---------|
| **Endpoint Prefix** | `/api/reviews` |
| **Subject type enum** | "product", "exam_type", "catalog_item" (backend) vs. frontend dropdown uses "exam_package" |
| **Subject specification** | exam_type requires `subject_text`; product/catalog_item require `subject_id` |
| **Credibility score** | Float (0.0-1.0); computed from order status, follow-up weight, rapid-repeat penalty |
| **Moderation** | is_pinned (surfaces review), is_collapsed (soft-hides body), store_id (scope) |
| **Images** | max 6 per review, max 5 MB each; MIME type validated; SHA-256 stored |
| **Anti-spam** | 1 review per order per 10 minutes |
| **Follow-ups** | 1 follow-up per initial review within 14 days |

### Packages

| Aspect | Details |
|--------|---------|
| **Endpoint Prefix** | `/api/packages` |
| **Versioning** | Package identified by `name`; version increments; only one `is_active=True` per name |
| **Immutability** | Old versions never modified; new version created on update |
| **Diff endpoint** | `GET /packages/{id}/diff/{other_id}` returns metadata_changes, items_added/removed/changed |
| **Diff response schema** | from_version, to_version, metadata_changes[], items_added[], items_removed[], items_changed[] |
| **Item snapshots** | PackageItem stores denormalized ExamItem fields at version creation for historical accuracy |

### Catalog

| Aspect | Details |
|--------|---------|
| **Endpoint Prefix** | `/api/catalog` |
| **Search/filter** | text search (name/description/category/origin), price range, stock range, shelf life range, harvest date range, active_only, in_stock |
| **Sorting** | name, price, stock_quantity, shelf_life_days, created_at, updated_at, harvest_date; asc/desc |
| **Attachments** | max 5 MB; allowed MIME: images (jpeg/png/webp/gif), PDF, Excel, Word, CSV, plain text |
| **File integrity** | SHA-256 fingerprint computed and stored; `GET .../verify` endpoint |
| **Upload path** | Served from `/uploads` endpoint; stored in `settings.attachments_path` |

### Frontend-Backend URL Mapping

| Feature | Frontend URL | Backend Endpoint |
|---------|-------------|-----------------|
| List notifications | `api.get('/notifications')` | `GET /api/notifications` |
| Unread count | `api.get('/notifications/unread-count')` | `GET /api/notifications/unread-count` |
| Messages inbox | `api.get('/messages/inbox')` | `GET /api/messages/inbox` |
| Create thread | `api.post('/messages/threads', payload)` | `POST /api/messages/threads` |
| Thread messages | `api.get('/messages/threads/{id}')` | `GET /api/messages/threads/{id}` |
| Post to thread | `api.post('/messages/threads/{id}/messages', {body})` | `POST /api/messages/threads/{id}/messages` |
| List reviews | `api.get('/reviews', {params: {subject_type, ...}})` | `GET /api/reviews?subject_type=...` |
| List catalog | `api.get('/catalog', {params: {search, category, ...}})` | `GET /api/catalog?search=...` |
| List packages | `api.get('/packages', {params: {active_only}})` | `GET /api/packages?active_only=true` |
| Package diff | `api.get('/packages/{id}/diff', {params: {base_id}})` | `GET /api/packages/{id}/diff?base_id=...` |

## 6. CRITICAL INTEGRATION POINTS

### Notification Store ↔ API

The frontend `notifications.js` store expects:
```javascript
// API response array format
[
  {
    id: 123,
    recipient_id: 1,
    notification_type: "info",     // "info" | "warning" | "error" | "success" | "system"
    event_subtype: "accepted",     // null or "accepted" | "arrived" | "completed" | "exception"
    title: "Order Accepted",
    body: "Your order #456 has been accepted.",
    is_read: false,
    status: "delivered",            // "pending" | "delivered" | "retrying" | "failed"
    related_entity_type: "order",   // null or "order" | "thread" | "message"
    related_entity_id: 456,
    created_at: "2026-04-08T12:34:56Z"
  },
  ...
]

// Preferences response format
{
  notify_order_accepted: true,
  notify_order_arrived: true,
  notify_order_completed: true,
  notify_order_exception: true,
  notify_new_message: true,
  notify_thread_reply: true,
  notify_system: true,
  notify_info: true,
}
```

### Review Subject Type Mismatch

**Backend enum:** "product", "exam_type", "catalog_item"
**Frontend dropdown options:** "Catalog", "Package" (maps to "exam_type"), "Service"

Frontend test code sends actual enum values; the dropdown may display differently but API expects backend enum strings.

### Package Diff Response

Frontend expects in `diff` object:
```javascript
{
  metadata_changes: [
    { field: "price", before: 99.99, after: 149.99 },
    { field: "validity_window_days", before: 30, after: 60 }
  ],
  items_added: [
    { exam_item_id: 5, code: "BUN", name: "Blood Urea Nitrogen" }
  ],
  items_removed: [...],
  items_changed: [
    {
      exam_item_id: 3,
      code: "ALB",
      name: "Albumin",
      changes: {  // NOTE: nested object with field diffs
        "is_required": { from: false, to: true }
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

Backend response uses `from_value`/`to_value` for metadata, but `changes` dict for item-level diffs.

---

## 7. SUMMARY TABLE: KEY ENDPOINTS

### Messages (`/api/messages`)
- `POST /` — send direct message
- `GET /inbox` — list inbox
- `GET /sent` — list sent
- `GET /inbox/unread-count` — badge
- `POST /threads` — create thread
- `GET /threads` — list threads
- `GET /threads/{id}` — get full thread
- `POST /threads/{id}/messages` — post to thread
- `PATCH /threads/{id}/read` — mark read

### Notifications (`/api/notifications`)
- `POST /` — create notification
- `POST /order-status` — emit order event
- `GET /` — list notifications
- `GET /unread-count` — badge
- `PATCH /{id}/read` — mark read
- `POST /mark-all-read` — bulk mark
- `GET /preferences/me` — get prefs
- `PUT /preferences/me` — update prefs
- `GET /admin/metrics` — delivery stats

### Reviews (`/api/reviews`)
- `POST /` — submit review
- `GET /` — list with filtering
- `POST /{id}/followup` — follow-up
- `POST /{id}/images` — upload
- `PATCH /{id}/pin` — pin
- `PATCH /{id}/collapse` — soft-hide
- `DELETE /{id}` — hard delete

### Catalog (`/api/catalog`)
- `POST /` — create item
- `GET /` — list with filtering/search
- `PUT /{id}` — update
- `DELETE /{id}` — delete
- `POST /{id}/attachments` — upload file
- `GET /{id}/attachments` — list files
- `GET /{id}/attachments/{att_id}` — download
- `GET /{id}/attachments/{att_id}/verify` — check integrity

### Packages (`/api/packages`)
- `POST /` — create version 1
- `GET /` — list
- `POST /{id}/new-version` — bump version
- `GET /{id}/diff/{other_id}` — compare versions
- `PATCH /{id}/activate` — set active
- `DELETE /{id}` — delete

---

## 8. STATIC FILE MOUNTS

**Location:** `/uploads` endpoint (mounted in main.py)
**Serves from:** `settings.attachments_path.parent` (typically `/uploads/catalog/` for catalog items)
**Usage:**
- Review images: `/uploads/{review_id}/{filename}`
- Catalog attachments: `/uploads/{item_id}/{filename}`

---

This is a fully offline-capable compliance portal with:
- End-to-end encrypted direct & threaded messaging
- Notification delivery with offline retry logic
- Review system with credibility scoring & moderation
- Versioned package management with diff capability
- Agricultural catalog with rich metadata & attachments
- Full RBAC enforcement across all endpoints

All data persists locally; no external API calls.

content.text:
1	"""
2	Messages & Conversation Threads
3	=================================
4	Two communication channels:
5	
6	1. Direct messages — point-to-point, Fernet-encrypted body.
7	2. Conversation threads — multi-participant, order-anchored, encrypted,
8	   with optional virtual-contact relay (masked aliases).
9	
10	Virtual-contact relay
11	---------------------
12	When a thread is created with use_virtual_ids=True, each participant is
13	assigned a random alias (e.g. "USR-A3F9").  API responses replace
14	sender_id with the sender's alias so participants cannot identify each
15	other's real accounts.  Moderators (admin) can always resolve aliases.
16	
17	Thread notifications
18	--------------------
19	Sending a thread message fires in-app notifications for all other
20	participants (subject to their preferences).
21	
22	RBAC
23	----
24	  Direct messages: all authenticated
25	  Threads: all authenticated (participants gate themselves)
26	  Virtual-contact resolve: admin only
27	"""
28	from __future__ import annotations
29	
30	from datetime import datetime, timezone
31	
32	from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException, Query, status
33	from sqlalchemy import func, select
34	from sqlalchemy.orm import Session, selectinload
35	
36	from app.core.dependencies import get_current_user, require_role
37	from app.core.encryption import encryptor
38	from app.core.notification_delivery import create_and_deliver, user_wants_notification
39	from app.database import get_db
40	from app.models.message import Message
41	from app.models.notification import NotificationType
42	from app.models.thread import Thread, ThreadMessage, ThreadParticipant, UserNotificationPreference
43	from app.models.user import User, UserRole
44	from app.schemas.message import (
45	    MessageCreate,
46	    MessageListResponse,
47	    MessageResponse,
48	    ThreadBrief,
49	    ThreadCreate,
50	    ThreadMessageCreate,
51	    ThreadMessageResponse,
52	    ThreadParticipantResponse,
53	    ThreadResponse,
54	    VirtualContactResponse,
55	)
56	
57	router = APIRouter(prefix="/messages", tags=["messages"])
58	
59	
60	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
61	# Internal helpers — direct messages
62	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
63	
64	def _get_msg_or_404(db: Session, msg_id: int, current_user: User) -> Message:
65	    msg = db.execute(select(Message).where(Message.id == msg_id)).scalar_one_or_none()
66	    if not msg:
67	        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, detail="Message not found")
68	    if msg.sender_id != current_user.id and msg.recipient_id != current_user.id:
69	        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN, detail="Access denied")
70	    return msg
71	
72	
73	def _decrypt_body(encrypted: str) -> str:
74	    try:
75	        return encryptor.decrypt(encrypted)
76	    except Exception:
77	        return "[decryption error]"
78	
79	
80	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
81	# Internal helpers — threads
82	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
83	
84	def _get_thread_or_404(db: Session, thread_id: int) -> Thread:
85	    thread = db.execute(
86	        select(Thread)
87	        .where(Thread.id == thread_id)
88	        .options(
89	            selectinload(Thread.participants),
90	            selectinload(Thread.messages),
91	        )
92	    ).scalar_one_or_none()
93	    if not thread:
94	        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, detail="Thread not found")
95	    return thread
96	
97	
98	def _assert_participant(thread: Thread, user_id: int) -> ThreadParticipant:
99	    for p in thread.participants:
100	        if p.user_id == user_id:
101	            return p
102	    raise HTTPException(
103	        status_code=status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN,
104	        detail="You are not a participant in this thread",
105	    )
106	
107	
108	def _sender_alias(thread: Thread, sender_id: int) -> str | None:
109	    """Return the sender's virtual alias if the thread uses masked IDs."""
110	    if not thread.use_virtual_ids:
111	        return None
112	    for p in thread.participants:
113	        if p.user_id == sender_id:
114	            return p.virtual_contact_id
115	    return None
116	
117	
118	def _build_thread_message_response(
119	    thread: Thread, tm: ThreadMessage
120	) -> ThreadMessageResponse:
121	    return ThreadMessageResponse(
122	        id=tm.id,
123	        thread_id=tm.thread_id,
124	        sender_id=tm.sender_id,
125	        sender_alias=_sender_alias(thread, tm.sender_id),
126	        body=None if tm.is_system_message else _decrypt_body(tm.body_encrypted),
127	        is_system_message=tm.is_system_message,
128	        created_at=tm.created_at,
129	    )
130	
131	
132	def _build_thread_response(thread: Thread, current_user_id: int) -> ThreadResponse:
133	    my_unread = 0
134	    for p in thread.participants:
135	        if p.user_id == current_user_id:
136	            my_unread = p.unread_count
137	            break
138	
139	    messages = [_build_thread_message_response(thread, tm) for tm in thread.messages]
140	    participants = [ThreadParticipantResponse.model_validate(p) for p in thread.participants]
141	
142	    return ThreadResponse(
143	        id=thread.id,
144	        subject=thread.subject,
145	        order_id=thread.order_id,
146	        created_by=thread.created_by,
147	        use_virtual_ids=thread.use_virtual_ids,
148	        is_archived=thread.is_archived,
149	        created_at=thread.created_at,
150	        updated_at=thread.updated_at,
151	        participants=participants,
152	        messages=messages,
153	        my_unread_count=my_unread,
154	    )
155	
156	
157	def _notify_thread_participants(
158	    thread: Thread,
159	    sender_id: int,
160	    db: Session,
161	) -> None:
162	    """Fire in-app notifications for all thread participants except the sender."""
163	    for p in thread.participants:
164	        if p.user_id == sender_id:
165	            continue
166	        if not user_wants_notification(p.user_id, NotificationType.info, None, db):
167	            continue
168	        create_and_deliver(
169	            db,
170	            recipient_id=p.user_id,
171	            notification_type=NotificationType.info,
172	            title=f"New message in: {thread.subject[:60]}",
173	            body=f"You have a new message in conversation '{thread.subject}'.",
174	            related_entity_type="thread",
175	            related_entity_id=thread.id,
176	        )
177	
178	
179	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
180	# Direct message endpoints
181	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
182	
183	@router.post("", response_model=MessageResponse, status_code=status.HTTP_201_CREATED)
184	def send_message(
185	    payload: MessageCreate,
186	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
187	    current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
188	):
189	    """Send a direct encrypted message to another user."""
190	    recipient = db.execute(
191	        select(User).where(User.id == payload.recipient_id, User.is_active == True)  # noqa: E712
192	    ).scalar_one_or_none()
193	    if not recipient:
194	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Recipient not found or inactive")
195	
196	    encrypted = encryptor.encrypt(payload.body)
197	    msg = Message(
198	        sender_id=current_user.id,
199	        recipient_id=payload.recipient_id,
200	        subject=payload.subject,
201	        body_encrypted=encrypted,
202	    )
203	    db.add(msg)
204	
205	    # Notify recipient if they want message notifications
206	    if user_wants_notification(payload.recipient_id, NotificationType.info, None, db):
207	        create_and_deliver(
208	            db,
209	            recipient_id=payload.recipient_id,
210	            notification_type=NotificationType.info,
211	            title=f"New message: {payload.subject[:60]}",
212	            body=f"You have a new message from {current_user.username}.",
213	            related_entity_type="message",
214	            related_entity_id=None,
215	        )
216	
217	    db.commit()
218	    db.refresh(msg)
219	    return MessageResponse(
220	        id=msg.id,
221	        sender_id=msg.sender_id,
222	        recipient_id=msg.recipient_id,
223	        subject=msg.subject,
224	        body=payload.body,
225	        is_read=msg.is_read,
226	        created_at=msg.created_at,
227	    )
228	
229	
230	@router.get("/inbox", response_model=list[MessageListResponse])
231	def get_inbox(
232	    unread_only: bool = Query(default=False),
233	    skip: int = Query(default=0, ge=0),
234	    limit: int = Query(default=50, ge=1, le=200),
235	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
236	    current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
237	):
238	    q = (
239	        select(Message)
240	        .where(Message.recipient_id == current_user.id)
241	        .order_by(Message.created_at.desc())
242	    )
243	    if unread_only:
244	        q = q.where(Message.is_read == False)  # noqa: E712
245	    return db.execute(q.offset(skip).limit(limit)).scalars().all()
246	
247	
248	@router.get("/sent", response_model=list[MessageListResponse])
249	def get_sent(
250	    skip: int = Query(default=0, ge=0),
251	    limit: int = Query(default=50, ge=1, le=200),
252	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
253	    current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
254	):
255	    msgs = db.execute(
256	        select(Message)
257	        .where(Message.sender_id == current_user.id)
258	        .order_by(Message.created_at.desc())
259	        .offset(skip)
260	        .limit(limit)
261	    ).scalars().all()
262	    return msgs
263	
264	
265	@router.get("/inbox/unread-count")
266	def inbox_unread_count(
267	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
268	    current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
269	):
270	    count = db.execute(
271	        select(func.count(Message.id)).where(
272	            Message.recipient_id == current_user.id,
273	            Message.is_read == False,  # noqa: E712
274	        )
275	    ).scalar_one()
276	    return {"unread_count": count}
277	
278	
279	@router.get("/{message_id}", response_model=MessageResponse)
280	def get_message(
281	    message_id: int,
282	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
283	    current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
284	):
285	    msg = _get_msg_or_404(db, message_id, current_user)
286	
287	    if msg.recipient_id == current_user.id and not msg.is_read:
288	        msg.is_read = True
289	        db.commit()
290	        db.refresh(msg)
291	
292	    return MessageResponse(
293	        id=msg.id,
294	        sender_id=msg.sender_id,
295	        recipient_id=msg.recipient_id,
296	        subject=msg.subject,
297	        body=_decrypt_body(msg.body_encrypted),
298	        is_read=msg.is_read,
299	        created_at=msg.created_at,
300	    )
301	
302	
303	@router.patch("/{message_id}/read", response_model=MessageResponse)
304	def mark_message_read(
305	    message_id: int,
306	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
307	    current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
308	):
309	    """Explicitly mark a message as read without decrypting the body."""
310	    msg = _get_msg_or_404(db, message_id, current_user)
311	    if msg.recipient_id != current_user.id:
312	        raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="Only the recipient can mark as read")
313	    if not msg.is_read:
314	        msg.is_read = True
315	        db.commit()
316	        db.refresh(msg)
317	    return MessageResponse(
318	        id=msg.id, sender_id=msg.sender_id, recipient_id=msg.recipient_id,
319	        subject=msg.subject, body=None, is_read=msg.is_read, created_at=msg.created_at,
320	    )
321	
322	
323	@router.delete("/{message_id}", status_code=status.HTTP_204_NO_CONTENT)
324	def delete_message(
325	    message_id: int,
326	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
327	    current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
328	):
329	    msg = _get_msg_or_404(db, message_id, current_user)
330	    db.delete(msg)
331	    db.commit()
332	
333	
334	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
335	# Thread endpoints
336	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
337	
338	@router.post("/threads", response_model=ThreadResponse, status_code=status.HTTP_201_CREATED)
339	def create_thread(
340	    payload: ThreadCreate,
341	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
342	    current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
343	):
344	    """
345	    Create a conversation thread.
346	
347	    - The creator is automatically added as a participant.
348	    - participant_ids must all map to active users.
349	    - When use_virtual_ids=True, every participant gets a random alias;
350	      senders appear in responses as that alias.
351	    - The initial_message is posted as the first ThreadMessage.
352	    """
353	    # Validate all participant IDs (include creator)
354	    all_ids = list({current_user.id} | set(payload.participant_ids))
355	    users_found = db.execute(
356	        select(User).where(User.id.in_(all_ids), User.is_active == True)  # noqa: E712
357	    ).scalars().all()
358	    found_ids = {u.id for u in users_found}
359	    missing = set(all_ids) - found_ids
360	    if missing:
361	        raise HTTPException(
362	            status_code=422,
363	            detail=f"User IDs not found or inactive: {sorted(missing)}",
364	        )
365	
366	    # Validate order if provided
367	    if payload.order_id is not None:
368	        from app.models.order import Order
369	        order = db.execute(select(Order).where(Order.id == payload.order_id)).scalar_one_or_none()
370	        if not order:
371	            raise HTTPException(status_code=422, detail=f"Order {payload.order_id} not found")
372	
373	    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
374	    thread = Thread(
375	        subject=payload.subject.strip(),
376	        order_id=payload.order_id,
377	        created_by=current_user.id,
378	        use_virtual_ids=payload.use_virtual_ids,
379	    )
380	    db.add(thread)
381	    db.flush()
382	
383	    # Add participants
384	    used_aliases: set[str] = set()
385	    for uid in all_ids:
386	        alias = None
387	        if payload.use_virtual_ids:
388	            # Ensure alias uniqueness within this thread
389	            while True:
390	                candidate = ThreadParticipant.generate_virtual_id()
391	                if candidate not in used_aliases:
392	                    alias = candidate
393	                    used_aliases.add(alias)
394	                    break
395	        participant = ThreadParticipant(
396	            thread_id=thread.id,
397	            user_id=uid,
398	            virtual_contact_id=alias,
399	            unread_count=0,
400	            joined_at=now,
401	        )
402	        db.add(participant)
403	
404	    db.flush()
405	
406	    # Post the initial message
407	    encrypted_body = encryptor.encrypt(payload.initial_message)
408	    first_msg = ThreadMessage(
409	        thread_id=thread.id,
410	        sender_id=current_user.id,
411	        body_encrypted=encrypted_body,
412	        is_system_message=False,
413	    )
414	    db.add(first_msg)
415	
416	    # Increment unread for everyone except the creator
417	    for p in db.execute(
418	        select(ThreadParticipant).where(
419	            ThreadParticipant.thread_id == thread.id,
420	            ThreadParticipant.user_id != current_user.id,
421	        )
422	    ).scalars().all():
423	        p.unread_count += 1
424	
425	    db.commit()
426	
427	    # Reload full thread for response
428	    thread = _get_thread_or_404(db, thread.id)
429	
430	    # Send notifications after commit
431	    _notify_thread_participants(thread, current_user.id, db)
432	    db.commit()
433	
434	    return _build_thread_response(thread, current_user.id)
435	
436	
437	@router.get("/threads", response_model=list[ThreadBrief])
438	def list_threads(
439	    archived: bool = Query(default=False),
440	    order_id: int | None = Query(default=None),
441	    skip: int = Query(default=0, ge=0),
442	    limit: int = Query(default=50, ge=1, le=200),
443	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
444	    current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
445	):
446	    """List threads where the current user is a participant."""
447	    # Get thread IDs where user participates
448	    participant_rows = db.execute(
449	        select(ThreadParticipant).where(ThreadParticipant.user_id == current_user.id)
450	    ).scalars().all()
451	    thread_ids = [p.thread_id for p in participant_rows]
452	    unread_map = {p.thread_id: p.unread_count for p in participant_rows}
453	
454	    if not thread_ids:
455	        return []
456	
457	    q = (
458	        select(Thread)
459	        .where(Thread.id.in_(thread_ids), Thread.is_archived == archived)
460	        .options(selectinload(Thread.participants), selectinload(Thread.messages))
461	    )
462	    if order_id is not None:
463	        q = q.where(Thread.order_id == order_id)
464	    q = q.order_by(Thread.updated_at.desc()).offset(skip).limit(limit)
465	
466	    threads = db.execute(q).scalars().all()
467	
468	    briefs = []
469	    for t in threads:
470	        last_msg = t.messages[-1].created_at if t.messages else None
471	        briefs.append(ThreadBrief(
472	            id=t.id,
473	            subject=t.subject,
474	            order_id=t.order_id,
475	            use_virtual_ids=t.use_virtual_ids,
476	            is_archived=t.is_archived,
477	            participant_count=len(t.participants),
478	            last_message_at=last_msg,
479	            my_unread_count=unread_map.get(t.id, 0),
480	            created_at=t.created_at,
481	        ))
482	    return briefs
483	
484	
485	@router.get("/threads/{thread_id}", response_model=ThreadResponse)
486	def get_thread(
487	    thread_id: int,
488	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
489	    current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
490	):
491	    """Get a thread with all messages (marks thread as read for current user)."""
492	    thread = _get_thread_or_404(db, thread_id)
493	    participant = _assert_participant(thread, current_user.id)
494	
495	    # Mark as read: reset unread_count and set last_read_at
496	    participant.unread_count = 0
497	    participant.last_read_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
498	    db.commit()
499	    db.refresh(thread)
500	
501	    thread = _get_thread_or_404(db, thread_id)
502	    return _build_thread_response(thread, current_user.id)
503	
504	
505	@router.post(
506	    "/threads/{thread_id}/messages",
507	    response_model=ThreadMessageResponse,
508	    status_code=status.HTTP_201_CREATED,
509	)
510	def send_thread_message(
511	    thread_id: int,
512	    payload: ThreadMessageCreate,
513	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
514	    current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
515	):
516	    """Post a message to a thread. Only participants may post."""
517	    thread = _get_thread_or_404(db, thread_id)
518	    _assert_participant(thread, current_user.id)
519	
520	    if thread.is_archived:
521	        raise HTTPException(status_code=409, detail="Cannot post to an archived thread")
522	
523	    encrypted = encryptor.encrypt(payload.body)
524	    tm = ThreadMessage(
525	        thread_id=thread_id,
526	        sender_id=current_user.id,
527	        body_encrypted=encrypted,
528	        is_system_message=False,
529	    )
530	    db.add(tm)
531	
532	    # Increment unread for other participants
533	    for p in thread.participants:
534	        if p.user_id != current_user.id:
535	            p.unread_count += 1
536	
537	    # Touch thread.updated_at for ordering
538	    thread.updated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
539	
540	    db.flush()
541	    db.commit()
542	    db.refresh(tm)
543	
544	    # Reload thread for alias resolution then notify
545	    thread = _get_thread_or_404(db, thread_id)
546	    _notify_thread_participants(thread, current_user.id, db)
547	    db.commit()
548	
549	    return ThreadMessageResponse(
550	        id=tm.id,
551	        thread_id=tm.thread_id,
552	        sender_id=tm.sender_id,
553	        sender_alias=_sender_alias(thread, current_user.id),
554	        body=payload.body,
555	        is_system_message=False,
556	        created_at=tm.created_at,
557	    )
558	
559	
560	@router.patch("/threads/{thread_id}/read", status_code=status.HTTP_204_NO_CONTENT)

565	):
566	    """Reset the unread counter for the current user in this thread."""

569	    participant.unread_count = 0
