1	"""
2	Notifications & Delivery Management
3	=====================================
4	In-app notifications with offline retry logic and per-user preferences.
5	
6	Delivery model
7	--------------
8	Notifications are written to the DB immediately.  The GET /notifications
9	endpoint triggers `process_due_retries()` at the start of each request
10	so any 'retrying' items that are now due are promoted to 'delivered'
11	before returning results.
12	
13	Order-status events (accepted / arrived / completed / exception) are
14	emitted via POST /notifications/order-status.  The system checks the
15	recipient's preferences before creating the row.
16	
17	RBAC
18	----
19	  Create (general): admin
20	  Emit order-status: admin, clinic_staff, catalog_manager
21	  Read own:         all authenticated
22	  Preferences:      self only
23	  Metrics:          admin only
24	"""
25	from __future__ import annotations
26	
27	from datetime import datetime, timezone
28	
29	from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException, Query, status
30	from sqlalchemy import select
31	from sqlalchemy.orm import Session
32	
33	from app.core.dependencies import get_current_user, require_role
34	from app.core.notification_delivery import (
35	    create_and_deliver,
36	    get_delivery_metrics,
37	    process_due_retries,
38	    user_wants_notification,
39	)
40	from app.database import get_db
41	from app.models.notification import Notification, NotificationStatus, NotificationType
42	from app.models.thread import UserNotificationPreference
43	from app.models.user import User
44	from app.schemas.notification import (
45	    DeliveryMetricsResponse,
46	    MarkReadRequest,
47	    NotificationBrief,
48	    NotificationCreate,
49	    NotificationPreferenceResponse,
50	    NotificationPreferenceUpdate,
51	    NotificationResponse,
52	    ORDER_STATUS_SUBTYPES as _SCHEMA_SUBTYPES,
53	    OrderStatusNotificationCreate,
54	)
55	
56	router = APIRouter(prefix="/notifications", tags=["notifications"])
57	
58	_ORDER_STATUS_TITLES = {
59	    "accepted":  "Order Accepted",
60	    "arrived":   "Order Arrived",
61	    "completed": "Order Completed",
62	    "exception": "Order Exception",
63	}
64	
65	
66	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
67	# Helpers
68	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
69	
70	def _get_or_404(db: Session, notif_id: int, current_user: User) -> Notification:
71	    n = db.execute(
72	        select(Notification).where(Notification.id == notif_id)
73	    ).scalar_one_or_none()
74	    if not n:
75	        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, detail="Notification not found")
76	    if n.recipient_id != current_user.id and current_user.role.value != "admin":
77	        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN, detail="Access denied")
78	    return n
79	
80	
81	def _get_or_create_prefs(db: Session, user_id: int) -> UserNotificationPreference:
82	    prefs = db.execute(
83	        select(UserNotificationPreference).where(UserNotificationPreference.user_id == user_id)
84	    ).scalar_one_or_none()
85	    if not prefs:
86	        prefs = UserNotificationPreference(user_id=user_id)
87	        db.add(prefs)
88	        db.flush()
89	    return prefs
90	
91	
92	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
93	# Notification CRUD
94	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
95	
96	@router.post("", response_model=NotificationResponse, status_code=status.HTTP_201_CREATED)
97	def create_notification(
98	    payload: NotificationCreate,
99	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
100	    _: User = Depends(require_role("admin")),
101	):
102	    """
103	    Create and immediately attempt delivery of an arbitrary notification.
104	    Preference check is skipped for admin-created notifications.
105	    """
106	    n = create_and_deliver(
107	        db,
108	        recipient_id=payload.recipient_id,
109	        notification_type=payload.notification_type,
110	        title=payload.title,
111	        body=payload.body,
112	        event_subtype=payload.event_subtype,
113	        related_entity_type=payload.related_entity_type,
114	        related_entity_id=payload.related_entity_id,
115	    )
116	    db.commit()
117	    db.refresh(n)
118	    return n
119	
120	
121	@router.post(
122	    "/order-status",
123	    response_model=NotificationResponse,
124	    status_code=status.HTTP_201_CREATED,
125	)
126	def emit_order_status_notification(
127	    payload: OrderStatusNotificationCreate,
128	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
129	    _: User = Depends(require_role("admin", "clinic_staff", "catalog_manager")),
130	):
131	    """
132	    Emit an order-status notification (accepted / arrived / completed / exception).
133	
134	    - Validates that event_subtype is a known order-status value.
135	    - Respects the recipient's notification preferences.
136	    - Creates and immediately attempts delivery.
137	    """
138	    if payload.event_subtype not in _SCHEMA_SUBTYPES:
139	        raise HTTPException(
140	            status_code=status.HTTP_422_UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY,
141	            detail=f"event_subtype must be one of: {sorted(_SCHEMA_SUBTYPES)}",
142	        )
143	
144	    # Preference check
145	    if not user_wants_notification(
146	        payload.recipient_id,
147	        NotificationType.info,
148	        payload.event_subtype,
149	        db,
150	    ):
151	        raise HTTPException(
152	            status_code=status.HTTP_422_UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY,
153	            detail=(
154	                f"Recipient has disabled '{payload.event_subtype}' notifications. "
155	                "Notification not created."
156	            ),
157	        )
158	
159	    title = _ORDER_STATUS_TITLES.get(payload.event_subtype, "Order Update")
160	    body = f"Your order #{payload.order_id} status: {payload.event_subtype.upper()}."
161	    if payload.extra_body:
162	        body = f"{body} {payload.extra_body}"
163	
164	    n = create_and_deliver(
165	        db,
166	        recipient_id=payload.recipient_id,
167	        notification_type=NotificationType.info,
168	        title=title,
169	        body=body,
170	        event_subtype=payload.event_subtype,
171	        related_entity_type="order",
172	        related_entity_id=payload.order_id,
173	    )
174	    db.commit()
175	    db.refresh(n)
176	    return n
177	
178	
179	@router.get("", response_model=list[NotificationBrief])
180	def list_notifications(
181	    unread_only: bool = Query(default=False),
182	    notification_type: NotificationType | None = Query(default=None),
183	    event_subtype: str | None = Query(default=None, max_length=50),
184	    skip: int = Query(default=0, ge=0),
185	    limit: int = Query(default=50, ge=1, le=200),
186	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
187	    current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
188	):
189	    """
190	    Fetch notifications for the authenticated user.
191	    Triggers the retry engine before returning results — any due retries
192	    are processed and committed so clients always see up-to-date statuses.
193	    """
194	    processed = process_due_retries(db)
195	    if processed:
196	        db.commit()
197	
198	    q = select(Notification).where(Notification.recipient_id == current_user.id)
199	    if unread_only:
200	        q = q.where(Notification.is_read == False)  # noqa: E712
201	    if notification_type is not None:
202	        q = q.where(Notification.notification_type == notification_type)
203	    if event_subtype is not None:
204	        q = q.where(Notification.event_subtype == event_subtype)
205	
206	    q = q.order_by(Notification.created_at.desc()).offset(skip).limit(limit)
207	    return db.execute(q).scalars().all()
208	
209	
210	@router.get("/unread-count")
211	def get_unread_count(
212	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
213	    current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
214	):
215	    """Returns the count of unread notifications — use for badge rendering."""
216	    from sqlalchemy import func
217	    count = db.execute(
218	        select(func.count(Notification.id)).where(
219	            Notification.recipient_id == current_user.id,
220	            Notification.is_read == False,  # noqa: E712
221	        )
222	    ).scalar_one()
223	    return {"unread_count": count}
224	
225	
226	@router.get("/{notif_id}", response_model=NotificationResponse)
227	def get_notification(
228	    notif_id: int,
229	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
230	    current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
231	):
232	    return _get_or_404(db, notif_id, current_user)
233	
234	
235	@router.patch("/{notif_id}/read", response_model=NotificationResponse)
236	def mark_notification_read(
237	    notif_id: int,
238	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
239	    current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
240	):
241	    """Mark a single notification as read."""
242	    n = _get_or_404(db, notif_id, current_user)
243	    if not n.is_read:
244	        n.is_read = True
245	        n.read_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
246	        db.commit()
247	        db.refresh(n)
248	    return n
249	
250	
251	@router.post("/mark-read", status_code=status.HTTP_204_NO_CONTENT)
252	def bulk_mark_read(
253	    payload: MarkReadRequest,
254	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
255	    current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
256	):
257	    """Mark a batch of notifications as read (only own notifications)."""
258	    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
259	    rows = db.execute(
260	        select(Notification).where(
261	            Notification.id.in_(payload.notification_ids),
262	            Notification.recipient_id == current_user.id,
263	            Notification.is_read == False,  # noqa: E712
264	        )
265	    ).scalars().all()
266	    for n in rows:
267	        n.is_read = True
268	        n.read_at = now
269	    db.commit()
270	
271	
272	@router.post("/mark-all-read", status_code=status.HTTP_204_NO_CONTENT)
273	def mark_all_read(
274	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
275	    current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
276	):
277	    """Mark every unread notification for the current user as read."""
278	    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
279	    rows = db.execute(
280	        select(Notification).where(
281	            Notification.recipient_id == current_user.id,
282	            Notification.is_read == False,  # noqa: E712
283	        )
284	    ).scalars().all()
285	    for n in rows:
286	        n.is_read = True
287	        n.read_at = now
288	    db.commit()
289	
290	
291	@router.delete("/{notif_id}", status_code=status.HTTP_204_NO_CONTENT)
292	def delete_notification(
293	    notif_id: int,
294	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
295	    current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
296	):
297	    """Delete (dismiss) a notification permanently."""
298	    n = _get_or_404(db, notif_id, current_user)
299	    db.delete(n)
300	    db.commit()
301	
302	
303	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
304	# Delivery metrics (admin only)
305	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
306	
307	@router.get("/admin/metrics", response_model=DeliveryMetricsResponse)
308	def delivery_metrics(
309	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
310	    _: User = Depends(require_role("admin")),
311	):
312	    """
313	    Aggregate delivery metrics computed from the notifications table.
314	    Offline-safe — no external monitoring required.
315	    """
316	    return get_delivery_metrics(db)
317	
318	
319	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
320	# Notification preferences (self)
321	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
322	
323	@router.get("/preferences/me", response_model=NotificationPreferenceResponse)
324	def get_my_preferences(
325	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
326	    current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
327	):
328	    """Retrieve the current user's notification subscription preferences."""
329	    prefs = _get_or_create_prefs(db, current_user.id)
330	    db.commit()
331	    db.refresh(prefs)
332	    return prefs
333	
334	
335	@router.put("/preferences/me", response_model=NotificationPreferenceResponse)
336	def update_my_preferences(
337	    payload: NotificationPreferenceUpdate,
338	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
339	    current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
340	):
341	    """
342	    Update notification subscription preferences.
343	    Only provided fields are changed; omitted fields retain their current values.
344	    """
345	    prefs = _get_or_create_prefs(db, current_user.id)
346	    update_data = payload.model_dump(exclude_unset=True)
347	    if not update_data:
348	        raise HTTPException(status_code=422, detail="No fields provided")
349	    for field, value in update_data.items():
350	        setattr(prefs, field, value)
351	    prefs.updated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
352	    db.commit()
353	    db.refresh(prefs)
354	    return prefs
355

1	"""
2	Packages (versioned exam bundles)
3	==================================
4	Manages exam packages — versioned, USD-priced bundles of exam items.
5	
6	Versioning contract
7	-------------------
8	- A package is identified by its *name*. Each structural change creates a new
9	  row with an incremented version number. Old rows are immutable.
10	- Only one version per name should be is_active=True at any time (the
11	  "current edition being sold"). Enforced by activate / deactivate endpoints.
12	- PackageItem rows store denormalised snapshots of the exam item's key fields
13	  at the moment the version is created, so historical records stay accurate
14	  even when ExamItem data is later updated.
15	
16	RBAC
17	----
18	  Write: admin, clinic_staff
19	  Read:  all authenticated users
20	"""
21	from __future__ import annotations
22	
23	from decimal import Decimal
24	from typing import Any
25	
26	from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException, Query, status
27	from sqlalchemy.orm import Session, selectinload
28	from sqlalchemy import select, func
29	
30	from app.database import get_db
31	from app.models.exam import ExamItem, Package, PackageItem
32	from app.models.user import User
33	from app.core.dependencies import get_current_user, require_role
34	from app.schemas.exam import (
35	    PackageCreate,
36	    PackageNewVersionRequest,
37	    PackageBrief,
38	    PackageResponse,
39	    PackageDiffResponse,
40	    DiffItem,
41	    ChangedItem,
42	    MetadataChange,
43	)

45	router = APIRouter(prefix="/packages", tags=["packages"])
46	
47	_WRITERS = ("admin", "clinic_staff")

49	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
50	# Internal helpers
51	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
52	
53	def _load_package(db: Session, package_id: int) -> Package:
54	    pkg = db.execute(
55	        select(Package)
56	        .where(Package.id == package_id)
57	        .options(selectinload(Package.items).selectinload(PackageItem.exam_item))
58	    ).scalar_one_or_none()
59	    if not pkg:
60	        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, detail="Package not found")
61	    return pkg
62	
63	
64	def _resolve_exam_items(
65	    db: Session,
66	    item_specs: list,
67	) -> dict[int, ExamItem]:
68	    """
69	    Fetch all requested ExamItems in one query and validate they exist and are active.
70	    Returns {exam_item_id: ExamItem}.
71	    """
72	    ids = [s.exam_item_id for s in item_specs]
73	    rows = db.execute(select(ExamItem).where(ExamItem.id.in_(ids))).scalars().all()
74	    found = {r.id: r for r in rows}
75	
76	    missing = set(ids) - set(found.keys())
77	    if missing:
78	        raise HTTPException(
79	            status_code=status.HTTP_422_UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY,
80	            detail=f"Exam item IDs not found: {sorted(missing)}",
81	        )
82	
83	    inactive = [eid for eid, ei in found.items() if not ei.is_active]
84	    if inactive:
85	        raise HTTPException(
86	            status_code=status.HTTP_422_UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY,
87	            detail=f"Exam items are inactive and cannot be added to a package: {sorted(inactive)}",
88	        )
89	    return found
90	
91	
92	def _build_ref_range_snapshot(ei: ExamItem) -> str | None:
93	    if ei.ref_range_text:
94	        return ei.ref_range_text
95	    lo, hi = ei.ref_range_min, ei.ref_range_max
96	    if lo is not None and hi is not None:
97	        return f"{lo} – {hi}"
98	    if lo is not None:
99	        return f">= {lo}"
100	    if hi is not None:
101	        return f"<= {hi}"
102	    return None
103	
104	
105	def _build_package_items(
106	    package: Package,
107	    item_specs: list,
108	    exam_items: dict[int, ExamItem],
109	) -> None:
110	    """Attach PackageItem rows (with snapshots) to *package*."""
111	    for spec in item_specs:
112	        ei = exam_items[spec.exam_item_id]
113	        pi = PackageItem(
114	            package_id=package.id,
115	            exam_item_id=ei.id,
116	            is_required=spec.is_required,
117	            item_code_snapshot=ei.code,
118	            item_name_snapshot=ei.name,
119	            unit_snapshot=ei.unit,
120	            ref_range_snapshot=_build_ref_range_snapshot(ei),
121	            collection_method_snapshot=ei.collection_method,
122	        )
123	        package.items.append(pi)
124	
125	
126	def _next_version(db: Session, name: str) -> int:
127	    max_ver = db.execute(
128	        select(func.max(Package.version)).where(Package.name == name)
129	    ).scalar()
130	    return (max_ver or 0) + 1
131	
132	
133	def _deactivate_other_versions(db: Session, name: str, keep_id: int) -> None:
134	    """Set is_active=False for all versions of *name* except *keep_id*."""
135	    others = db.execute(
136	        select(Package).where(Package.name == name, Package.id != keep_id)
137	    ).scalars().all()
138	    for pkg in others:
139	        pkg.is_active = False
140	
141	
142	def _package_to_brief(pkg: Package) -> PackageBrief:
143	    return PackageBrief(
144	        id=pkg.id,
145	        name=pkg.name,
146	        version=pkg.version,
147	        price=pkg.price,
148	        validity_window_days=pkg.validity_window_days,
149	        is_active=pkg.is_active,
150	        created_at=pkg.created_at,
151	        item_count=len(pkg.items),
152	    )
153

155	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
156	# Diff logic
157	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
158	
159	def _compute_diff(pkg_a: Package, pkg_b: Package) -> PackageDiffResponse:
160	    # Metadata fields to compare
161	    meta_fields = ["name", "description", "price", "validity_window_days"]
162	    meta_changes: list[MetadataChange] = []
163	    for field in meta_fields:
164	        va = getattr(pkg_a, field)
165	        vb = getattr(pkg_b, field)
166	        # Normalise Decimal for comparison
167	        if isinstance(va, Decimal):
168	            va = float(va)
169	        if isinstance(vb, Decimal):
170	            vb = float(vb)
171	        if va != vb:
172	            meta_changes.append(MetadataChange(field=field, from_value=va, to_value=vb))
173	
174	    # Index items by exam_item_id
175	    a_items: dict[int, PackageItem] = {pi.exam_item_id: pi for pi in pkg_a.items}
176	    b_items: dict[int, PackageItem] = {pi.exam_item_id: pi for pi in pkg_b.items}
177	
178	    a_ids = set(a_items)
179	    b_ids = set(b_items)
180	
181	    added: list[DiffItem] = []
182	    for eid in b_ids - a_ids:
183	        pi = b_items[eid]
184	        added.append(DiffItem(
185	            exam_item_id=eid,
186	            code=pi.item_code_snapshot,
187	            name=pi.item_name_snapshot,
188	        ))
189	
190	    removed: list[DiffItem] = []
191	    for eid in a_ids - b_ids:
192	        pi = a_items[eid]
193	        removed.append(DiffItem(
194	            exam_item_id=eid,
195	            code=pi.item_code_snapshot,
196	            name=pi.item_name_snapshot,
197	        ))
198	
199	    changed: list[ChangedItem] = []
200	    for eid in a_ids & b_ids:
201	        pa, pb = a_items[eid], b_items[eid]
202	        item_changes: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
203	        snapshot_fields = [
204	            "is_required",
205	            "item_code_snapshot",
206	            "item_name_snapshot",
207	            "unit_snapshot",
208	            "ref_range_snapshot",
209	            "collection_method_snapshot",
210	        ]
211	        for f in snapshot_fields:
212	            va, vb = getattr(pa, f), getattr(pb, f)
213	            if va != vb:
214	                item_changes[f] = {"from": va, "to": vb}
215	        if item_changes:
216	            changed.append(ChangedItem(
217	                exam_item_id=eid,
218	                code=pb.item_code_snapshot,
219	                name=pb.item_name_snapshot,
220	                changes=item_changes,
221	            ))
222	
223	    return PackageDiffResponse(
224	        from_version=pkg_a.version,
225	        to_version=pkg_b.version,
226	        metadata_changes=meta_changes,
227	        items_added=sorted(added, key=lambda x: x.code),
228	        items_removed=sorted(removed, key=lambda x: x.code),
229	        items_changed=sorted(changed, key=lambda x: x.code),
230	    )
231

237	@router.post("", response_model=PackageResponse, status_code=status.HTTP_201_CREATED)
238	def create_package(
239	    payload: PackageCreate,
240	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
241	    current_user: User = Depends(require_role(*_WRITERS)),
242	):
243	    """
244	    Create package version 1.
245	    The package starts as inactive; call /activate to make it the
246	    edition that is currently sold.
247	    """
248	    # Check name doesn't already exist
249	    existing = db.execute(
250	        select(Package).where(Package.name == payload.name)
251	    ).scalar_one_or_none()
252	    if existing:
253	        raise HTTPException(
254	            status_code=status.HTTP_409_CONFLICT,
255	            detail=(
256	                f"Package '{payload.name}' already exists "
257	                f"(latest version={existing.version}). "
258	                "Use POST /packages/{id}/new-version to add a new version."
259	            ),
260	        )
261	
262	    exam_items = _resolve_exam_items(db, payload.items)
263	
264	    pkg = Package(
265	        name=payload.name.strip(),
266	        description=payload.description,
267	        version=1,
268	        price=payload.price,
269	        validity_window_days=payload.validity_window_days,
270	        is_active=False,
271	        created_by=current_user.id,
272	    )
273	    db.add(pkg)
274	    db.flush()  # get pkg.id before building items
275	
276	    _build_package_items(pkg, payload.items, exam_items)
277	
278	    db.commit()
279	    db.refresh(pkg)
280	    return pkg

283	@router.get("", response_model=list[PackageBrief])
284	def list_packages(
285	    active_only: bool = Query(default=False, description="Only show is_active=True versions"),
286	    name: str | None = Query(default=None, max_length=255, description="Filter by package name"),
287	    skip: int = Query(default=0, ge=0),
288	    limit: int = Query(default=100, ge=1, le=500),
289	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
290	    _: User = Depends(get_current_user),
291	):
292	    """
293	    List packages.  By default returns all versions of all packages.
294	    Use active_only=true to see only the editions currently being sold.
295	    """
296	    q = select(Package).options(selectinload(Package.items))
297	    if active_only:
298	        q = q.where(Package.is_active == True)  # noqa: E712
299	    if name:
300	        q = q.where(Package.name.ilike(f"%{name}%"))
301	    q = q.order_by(Package.name, Package.version.desc()).offset(skip).limit(limit)
302	    pkgs = db.execute(q).scalars().all()
303	    return [_package_to_brief(p) for p in pkgs]

316	@router.get("/{package_id}/versions", response_model=list[PackageBrief])
317	def list_package_versions(
318	    package_id: int,
319	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
320	    _: User = Depends(require_role(*_WRITERS)),
321	):
322	    """
323	    Return every version of the same package name (identified by package_id).
324	    Useful to see the version history before selecting which edition to activate.
325	    """
326	    pkg = _load_package(db, package_id)
327	    siblings = db.execute(
328	        select(Package)
329	        .where(Package.name == pkg.name)
330	        .options(selectinload(Package.items))
331	        .order_by(Package.version)
332	    ).scalars().all()
333	    return [_package_to_brief(p) for p in siblings]

336	@router.post("/{package_id}/new-version", response_model=PackageResponse, status_code=status.HTTP_201_CREATED)
337	def create_new_version(
338	    package_id: int,
339	    payload: PackageNewVersionRequest,
340	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
341	    current_user: User = Depends(require_role(*_WRITERS)),
342	):
343	    """
344	    Bump a package to a new version by providing what changed.
345	
346	    Rules:
347	    - The original version row is never modified (immutable history).
348	    - A new row is inserted with version = max(existing) + 1.
349	    - Fields not supplied in the payload carry over from the source version.
350	    - At least one field must differ from the source.
351	    - The new version starts inactive; activate it explicitly when ready.
352	    """
353	    source = _load_package(db, package_id)
354	
355	    # Resolve new items or carry over from source
356	    new_item_specs = payload.items if payload.items is not None else [
357	        type("Spec", (), {"exam_item_id": pi.exam_item_id, "is_required": pi.is_required})()
358	        for pi in source.items
359	    ]
360	    if not new_item_specs:
361	        raise HTTPException(
362	            status_code=status.HTTP_422_UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY,
363	            detail="A package must contain at least one item",
364	        )
365	
366	    exam_items = _resolve_exam_items(db, new_item_specs)
367	
368	    # Determine effective values (carry over if not supplied)
369	    new_description = payload.description if payload.description is not None else source.description
370	    new_price = payload.price if payload.price is not None else source.price
371	    new_validity = payload.validity_window_days if payload.validity_window_days is not None else source.validity_window_days
372	
373	    # Guard: require at least one actual change
374	    source_item_map = {pi.exam_item_id: pi.is_required for pi in source.items}
375	    new_item_map = {s.exam_item_id: s.is_required for s in new_item_specs}
376	
377	    nothing_changed = (
378	        new_description == source.description
379	        and Decimal(str(new_price)) == Decimal(str(source.price))
380	        and new_validity == source.validity_window_days
381	        and source_item_map == new_item_map
382	    )
383	    if nothing_changed:
384	        raise HTTPException(
385	            status_code=status.HTTP_422_UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY,
386	            detail="No changes detected. The new version would be identical to the source.",
387	        )
388	
389	    next_ver = _next_version(db, source.name)
390	
391	    new_pkg = Package(
392	        name=source.name,
393	        description=new_description,
394	        version=next_ver,
395	        price=new_price,
396	        validity_window_days=new_validity,
397	        is_active=False,
398	        created_by=current_user.id,
399	    )
400	    db.add(new_pkg)
401	    db.flush()
402	
403	    _build_package_items(new_pkg, new_item_specs, exam_items)
404	
405	    db.commit()
406	    db.refresh(new_pkg)
407	    return new_pkg
408

410	@router.get("/{package_id}/diff/{other_id}", response_model=PackageDiffResponse)
411	def diff_versions(
412	    package_id: int,
413	    other_id: int,
414	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
415	    _: User = Depends(require_role(*_WRITERS)),
416	):
417	    """
418	    Show what changed between two package versions.
419	
420	    Returns metadata changes (name, price, validity) plus item-level
421	    additions, removals, and modifications (e.g. required → optional).
422	
423	    Tip: package_id = older version, other_id = newer version to read
424	    the diff in chronological order.
425	    """
426	    if package_id == other_id:
427	        raise HTTPException(
428	            status_code=status.HTTP_422_UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY,
429	            detail="package_id and other_id must be different",
430	        )
431	    pkg_a = _load_package(db, package_id)
432	    pkg_b = _load_package(db, other_id)
433	
434	    # Both must belong to the same package name for a meaningful diff
435	    if pkg_a.name != pkg_b.name:
436	        raise HTTPException(
437	            status_code=status.HTTP_422_UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY,
438	            detail=(
439	                f"Packages belong to different names "
440	                f"('{pkg_a.name}' vs '{pkg_b.name}'). "
441	                "Cross-package diff is not supported."
442	            ),
443	        )
444	
445	    return _compute_diff(pkg_a, pkg_b)
446

448	@router.patch("/{package_id}/activate", response_model=PackageResponse)
449	def activate_package(
450	    package_id: int,
451	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
452	    _: User = Depends(require_role(*_WRITERS)),
453	):
454	    """
455	    Mark this version as the active edition being sold.
456	
457	    All other versions of the same package name are automatically
458	    deactivated so only one edition is ever "current" at a time.
459	
460	    This is the confirmation step that a specific version is correct
461	    and should be offered to patients / customers.
462	    """
463	    pkg = _load_package(db, package_id)
464	
465	    if pkg.is_active:
466	        raise HTTPException(
467	            status_code=status.HTTP_409_CONFLICT,
468	            detail="Package version is already active",
469	        )
470	    if not pkg.items:
471	        raise HTTPException(
472	            status_code=status.HTTP_422_UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY,
473	            detail="Cannot activate a package with no items",
474	        )
475	
476	    _deactivate_other_versions(db, pkg.name, keep_id=pkg.id)
477	    pkg.is_active = True
478	
479	    db.commit()
480	    db.refresh(pkg)
481	    return pkg

484	@router.patch("/{package_id}/deactivate", response_model=PackageResponse)
485	def deactivate_package(
486	    package_id: int,
487	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
488	    _: User = Depends(require_role("admin")),
489	):
490	    """
491	    Deactivate a package version (admin only).
492	    The package is no longer offered for new orders but historical records
493	    that reference it are unaffected.
494	    """
495	    pkg = _load_package(db, package_id)
496	
497	    if not pkg.is_active:
498	        raise HTTPException(
499	            status_code=status.HTTP_409_CONFLICT,
500	            detail="Package version is already inactive",
501	        )
502	
503	    pkg.is_active = False
504	    db.commit()

509	@router.post("/{package_id}/items", response_model=PackageResponse, status_code=status.HTTP_201_CREATED)
510	def add_item_to_package(
511	    package_id: int,
512	    payload: "PackageItemAddRequest",
513	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
514	    current_user: User = Depends(require_role(*_WRITERS)),
515	):
516	    """
517	    Add an exam item to a package by creating a new version automatically.
518	    The new version is a copy of the source with the extra item appended.
519	    The new version starts inactive.
520	    """
521	    from app.schemas.exam import PackageItemIn  # local import avoids circular
522	
523	    source = _load_package(db, package_id)
524	
525	    # Check item isn't already in this version
526	    existing_ids = {pi.exam_item_id for pi in source.items}
527	    if payload.exam_item_id in existing_ids:
528	        raise HTTPException(
529	            status_code=status.HTTP_409_CONFLICT,
530	            detail=f"Exam item {payload.exam_item_id} is already in package version {source.version}",
531	        )
532	
533	    new_specs = [
534	        PackageItemIn(exam_item_id=pi.exam_item_id, is_required=pi.is_required)
535	        for pi in source.items
536	    ] + [PackageItemIn(exam_item_id=payload.exam_item_id, is_required=payload.is_required)]
537	
538	    exam_items = _resolve_exam_items(db, new_specs)
539	    next_ver = _next_version(db, source.name)
540	
541	    new_pkg = Package(
542	        name=source.name,
543	        description=source.description,
544	        version=next_ver,
545	        price=source.price,
546	        validity_window_days=source.validity_window_days,
547	        is_active=False,
548	        created_by=current_user.id,
549	    )
550	    db.add(new_pkg)
551	    db.flush()
552	    _build_package_items(new_pkg, new_specs, exam_items)
553	    db.commit()
554	    db.refresh(new_pkg)
555	    return new_pkg

149	router.beforeEach(async (to, from, next) => {
150	  const authStore = useAuthStore()
151	
152	  if (!authStore.user && authStore.isAuthenticated) {
153	    await authStore.init()
154	  }
155	
156	  if (to.meta.requiresAuth && !authStore.isAuthenticated) {
157	    return next({ name: 'Login', query: { redirect: to.fullPath } })
158	  }
159	  if (to.meta.requiresGuest && authStore.isAuthenticated) {
160	    return next({ name: 'Dashboard' })
161	  }
162	  if (to.meta.roles && authStore.user && !to.meta.roles.includes(authStore.userRole)) {
163	    return next({ name: 'Dashboard' })
164	  }
165	
166	  next()
167	})
168	
169	export default router

1	"""
2	Agricultural Catalog Module
3	============================
4	Manages catalog entries for agricultural products including grade, origin,
5	harvest batch, packaging specs, shelf life, and file attachments.
6	
7	File attachments are stored on the local filesystem (offline-safe):
8	  - MIME type validated against an allow-list
9	  - Magic bytes sniffed to catch spoofed extensions
10	  - 5 MB size limit enforced before disk write
11	  - SHA-256 fingerprint computed and stored for offline integrity checks
12	  - Files served directly by FastAPI (no cloud/CDN)
13	
14	RBAC
15	----
16	  Write (create, update, delete, upload): admin, catalog_manager
17	  Stock set (absolute):                   admin only
18	  Read / download:                        all authenticated users
19	"""
20	from __future__ import annotations
21	
22	import uuid
23	from datetime import datetime, timezone
24	from pathlib import Path
25	
26	from fastapi import (
27	    APIRouter, Depends, File, HTTPException, Query, Request,
28	    UploadFile, status,
29	)
30	from fastapi.responses import FileResponse
31	from sqlalchemy import select, func, or_
32	from sqlalchemy.orm import Session, selectinload
33	
34	from app.config import settings
35	from app.core.dependencies import get_current_user, require_role
36	from app.core.file_utils import ValidationError, validate_upload, verify_file_integrity
37	from app.database import get_db
38	from app.models.catalog import (
39	    CatalogAttachment,
40	    CatalogItem,
41	    ALLOWED_MIME_TYPES,
42	    MAX_ATTACHMENT_SIZE_BYTES,
43	)
44	from app.models.user import User
45	from app.schemas.catalog import (
46	    AttachmentVerifyResponse,
47	    CatalogAttachmentResponse,
48	    CatalogItemBrief,
49	    CatalogItemCreate,
50	    CatalogItemResponse,
51	    CatalogItemUpdate,
52	    CatalogSortField,
53	    SortDir,
54	    StockAdjust,
55	    StockSet,
56	)
57	
58	router = APIRouter(prefix="/catalog", tags=["catalog"])
59	
60	_WRITERS = ("admin", "catalog_manager")
61

63	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
64	# Internal helpers
65	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
66	
67	def _get_item_or_404(db: Session, item_id: int, *, with_attachments: bool = False) -> CatalogItem:
68	    q = select(CatalogItem).where(CatalogItem.id == item_id)
69	    if with_attachments:
70	        q = q.options(selectinload(CatalogItem.attachments))
71	    item = db.execute(q).scalar_one_or_none()
72	    if not item:
73	        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, detail="Catalog item not found")
74	    return item
75	
76	
77	def _get_attachment_or_404(db: Session, item_id: int, att_id: int) -> CatalogAttachment:
78	    att = db.execute(
79	        select(CatalogAttachment).where(
80	            CatalogAttachment.id == att_id,
81	            CatalogAttachment.catalog_item_id == item_id,
82	        )
83	    ).scalar_one_or_none()
84	    if not att:
85	        raise HTTPException(
86	            status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND,
87	            detail=f"Attachment {att_id} not found on catalog item {item_id}",
88	        )
89	    return att
90	
91	
92	def _item_dir(item_id: int) -> Path:
93	    """Return (and create) the per-item upload directory."""
94	    d = settings.attachments_path / str(item_id)
95	    d.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
96	    return d
97	
98	
99	def _to_brief(item: CatalogItem, attachment_count: int) -> CatalogItemBrief:
100	    return CatalogItemBrief(
101	        id=item.id,
102	        name=item.name,
103	        category=item.category,
104	        price=item.price,
105	        stock_quantity=item.stock_quantity,
106	        grade=item.grade,
107	        origin=item.origin,
108	        harvest_batch=item.harvest_batch,
109	        shelf_life_days=item.shelf_life_days,
110	        is_active=item.is_active,
111	        created_at=item.created_at,
112	        updated_at=item.updated_at,
113	        attachment_count=attachment_count,
114	    )
115	
116	
117	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
118	# CRUD endpoints
119	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
120	
121	@router.post("", response_model=CatalogItemResponse, status_code=status.HTTP_201_CREATED)
122	def create_item(
123	    payload: CatalogItemCreate,
124	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
125	    current_user: User = Depends(require_role(*_WRITERS)),
126	):
127	    """Create a new agricultural catalog entry."""
128	    item = CatalogItem(
129	        name=payload.name.strip(),
130	        description=payload.description,
131	        category=payload.category,
132	        price=payload.price,
133	        stock_quantity=payload.stock_quantity,
134	        grade=payload.grade,
135	        specifications=payload.specifications,
136	        origin=payload.origin,
137	        harvest_batch=payload.harvest_batch,
138	        harvest_date=payload.harvest_date,
139	        packaging_info=payload.packaging_info,
140	        shelf_life_days=payload.shelf_life_days,
141	        is_active=True,
142	        created_by=current_user.id,
143	    )
144	    db.add(item)
145	    db.commit()
146	    db.refresh(item)
147	    # Reload with attachments relationship
148	    return _get_item_or_404(db, item.id, with_attachments=True)
149	
150	
151	@router.get("", response_model=list[CatalogItemBrief])
152	def list_items(
153	    # --- text search ---
154	    search: str | None = Query(default=None, max_length=200,
155	                                description="Full-text search across name, description, category, origin"),
156	    name: str | None = Query(default=None, max_length=255),
157	    category: str | None = Query(default=None, max_length=100),
158	    grade: str | None = Query(default=None, max_length=50),
159	    origin: str | None = Query(default=None, max_length=255),
160	    harvest_batch: str | None = Query(default=None, max_length=100),
161	
162	    # --- numeric range filters ---
163	    price_min: float | None = Query(default=None, ge=0),
164	    price_max: float | None = Query(default=None, ge=0),
165	    stock_min: int | None = Query(default=None, ge=0),
166	    stock_max: int | None = Query(default=None, ge=0),
167	    shelf_life_min: int | None = Query(default=None, ge=1),
168	    shelf_life_max: int | None = Query(default=None, ge=1),
169	
170	    # --- date range ---
171	    harvest_date_from: datetime | None = Query(default=None),
172	    harvest_date_to: datetime | None = Query(default=None),
173	
174	    # --- boolean flags ---
175	    active_only: bool = Query(default=True),
176	    in_stock: bool = Query(default=False, description="Only items with stock_quantity > 0"),
177	
178	    # --- sorting ---
179	    sort_by: CatalogSortField = Query(default=CatalogSortField.name),
180	    sort_dir: SortDir = Query(default=SortDir.asc),
181	
182	    # --- pagination ---
183	    skip: int = Query(default=0, ge=0),
184	    limit: int = Query(default=100, ge=1, le=500),
185	
186	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
187	    _: User = Depends(get_current_user),
188	):
189	    """
190	    List catalog items with multi-criteria filtering.
191	
192	    All filters are AND-combined.  `search` does a case-insensitive OR
193	    across name, description, category, and origin.
194	    """
195	    q = select(CatalogItem)
196	
197	    # Boolean flags
198	    if active_only:
199	        q = q.where(CatalogItem.is_active == True)  # noqa: E712
200	    if in_stock:
201	        q = q.where(CatalogItem.stock_quantity > 0)
202	
203	    # Text filters
204	    if search:
205	        term = f"%{search}%"
206	        q = q.where(
207	            or_(
208	                CatalogItem.name.ilike(term),
209	                CatalogItem.description.ilike(term),
210	                CatalogItem.category.ilike(term),
211	                CatalogItem.origin.ilike(term),
212	            )
213	        )
214	    if name:
215	        q = q.where(CatalogItem.name.ilike(f"%{name}%"))
216	    if category:
217	        q = q.where(CatalogItem.category.ilike(f"%{category}%"))
218	    if grade:
219	        q = q.where(CatalogItem.grade.ilike(f"%{grade}%"))
220	    if origin:
221	        q = q.where(CatalogItem.origin.ilike(f"%{origin}%"))
222	    if harvest_batch:
223	        q = q.where(CatalogItem.harvest_batch.ilike(f"%{harvest_batch}%"))
224	
225	    # Numeric ranges
226	    if price_min is not None:
227	        q = q.where(CatalogItem.price >= price_min)
228	    if price_max is not None:
229	        q = q.where(CatalogItem.price <= price_max)
230	    if stock_min is not None:
231	        q = q.where(CatalogItem.stock_quantity >= stock_min)
232	    if stock_max is not None:
233	        q = q.where(CatalogItem.stock_quantity <= stock_max)
234	    if shelf_life_min is not None:
235	        q = q.where(CatalogItem.shelf_life_days >= shelf_life_min)
236	    if shelf_life_max is not None:
237	        q = q.where(CatalogItem.shelf_life_days <= shelf_life_max)
238	
239	    # Date ranges
240	    if harvest_date_from is not None:
241	        q = q.where(CatalogItem.harvest_date >= harvest_date_from)
242	    if harvest_date_to is not None:
243	        q = q.where(CatalogItem.harvest_date <= harvest_date_to)
244	
245	    # Validate range pairs
246	    if price_min is not None and price_max is not None and price_min > price_max:
247	        raise HTTPException(status_code=422, detail="price_min must be <= price_max")
248	    if stock_min is not None and stock_max is not None and stock_min > stock_max:
249	        raise HTTPException(status_code=422, detail="stock_min must be <= stock_max")
250	    if shelf_life_min is not None and shelf_life_max is not None and shelf_life_min > shelf_life_max:
251	        raise HTTPException(status_code=422, detail="shelf_life_min must be <= shelf_life_max")
252	
253	    # Sorting
254	    sort_col = getattr(CatalogItem, sort_by.value)
255	    q = q.order_by(sort_col.asc() if sort_dir == SortDir.asc else sort_col.desc())
256	
257	    # Attachment counts in a single subquery to avoid N+1
258	    att_counts_rows = db.execute(
259	        select(
260	            CatalogAttachment.catalog_item_id,
261	            func.count(CatalogAttachment.id).label("cnt"),
262	        ).group_by(CatalogAttachment.catalog_item_id)
263	    ).all()
264	    att_counts: dict[int, int] = {row.catalog_item_id: row.cnt for row in att_counts_rows}
265	
266	    items = db.execute(q.offset(skip).limit(limit)).scalars().all()
267	    return [_to_brief(it, att_counts.get(it.id, 0)) for it in items]
268

280	@router.put("/{item_id}", response_model=CatalogItemResponse)
281	def update_item(
282	    item_id: int,
283	    payload: CatalogItemUpdate,
284	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
285	    _: User = Depends(require_role(*_WRITERS)),
286	):
287	    """Full or partial update of a catalog entry (all fields optional)."""
288	    item = _get_item_or_404(db, item_id)
289	
290	    update_data = payload.model_dump(exclude_unset=True)
291	    if not update_data:
292	        raise HTTPException(status_code=422, detail="No fields provided for update")
293	
294	    for field, value in update_data.items():
295	        setattr(item, field, value)
296	    item.updated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
297	
298	    db.commit()
299	    db.refresh(item)
300	    return _get_item_or_404(db, item_id, with_attachments=True)
301

303	@router.patch("/{item_id}/deactivate", response_model=CatalogItemResponse)
304	def deactivate_item(
305	    item_id: int,
306	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
307	    _: User = Depends(require_role(*_WRITERS)),
308	):
309	    """
310	    Soft-delete: mark item as inactive.
311	    Preserves all data and attachments; item no longer appears in default listings.
312	    """
313	    item = _get_item_or_404(db, item_id)
314	    if not item.is_active:
315	        raise HTTPException(status_code=409, detail="Item is already inactive")
316	    item.is_active = False
317	    item.updated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
318	    db.commit()
319	    db.refresh(item)
320	    return _get_item_or_404(db, item_id, with_attachments=True)
321

324	def reactivate_item(
325	    item_id: int,
326	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
327	    _: User = Depends(require_role(*_WRITERS)),
328	):
329	    """Re-enable a previously deactivated catalog item."""
330	    item = _get_item_or_404(db, item_id)
331	    if item.is_active:
332	        raise HTTPException(status_code=409, detail="Item is already active")
333	    item.is_active = True
334	    item.updated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
335	    db.commit()

340	@router.delete("/{item_id}", status_code=status.HTTP_204_NO_CONTENT)
341	def delete_item(
342	    item_id: int,
343	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
344	    _: User = Depends(require_role("admin")),
345	):
346	    """
347	    Hard-delete a catalog item and all its attachments (admin only).
348	    Attachment files are removed from disk.
349	    """
350	    item = _get_item_or_404(db, item_id, with_attachments=True)
351	
352	    # Remove all attachment files from disk first
353	    item_dir = settings.attachments_path / str(item_id)
354	    for att in item.attachments:
355	        file_path = item_dir / att.stored_filename
356	        if file_path.exists():
357	            file_path.unlink(missing_ok=True)
358	    if item_dir.exists():
359	        try:
360	            item_dir.rmdir()  # only removes if empty
361	        except OSError:
362	            pass  # non-empty dir (unexpected leftover files) — leave it
363	
364	    db.delete(item)
365	    db.commit()

372	@router.put("/{item_id}/stock", response_model=CatalogItemResponse)
373	def adjust_stock(
374	    item_id: int,
375	    payload: StockAdjust,
376	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
377	    _: User = Depends(require_role(*_WRITERS)),
378	):
379	    """
380	    Adjust stock by a relative delta (positive = restock, negative = consume).
381	    Rejects adjustments that would make stock negative.
382	    """
383	    item = _get_item_or_404(db, item_id)
384	    new_stock = item.stock_quantity + payload.adjustment
385	    if new_stock < 0:
386	        raise HTTPException(
387	            status_code=422,
388	            detail=(
389	                f"Adjustment of {payload.adjustment} would result in negative stock "
390	                f"(current={item.stock_quantity})"
391	            ),
392	        )
393	    item.stock_quantity = new_stock
394	    item.updated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
395	    db.commit()
396	    db.refresh(item)
397	    return _get_item_or_404(db, item_id, with_attachments=True)

425	async def upload_attachment(
426	    item_id: int,
427	    file: UploadFile = File(...),
428	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
429	    current_user: User = Depends(require_role(*_WRITERS)),
430	):
431	    """
432	    Upload a file attachment to a catalog entry.
433	
434	    Validation (fully offline):
435	      - MIME type must be in the allow-list
436	      - Magic bytes must match the declared Content-Type
437	      - File must be <= 5 MB
438	      - File must not be empty
439	
440	    The SHA-256 fingerprint is computed from the raw bytes and stored
441	    alongside the file metadata for later integrity verification.
442	    """
443	    item = _get_item_or_404(db, item_id)
444	
445	    # Read file into memory for validation (max 5 MB is safe)
446	    data = await file.read()
447	    declared_mime = file.content_type or "application/octet-stream"
448	    original_filename = file.filename or "upload"
449	
450	    try:
451	        fingerprint = validate_upload(
452	            data=data,
453	            declared_mime=declared_mime,
454	            original_filename=original_filename,
455	            allowed_mimes=ALLOWED_MIME_TYPES,
456	            max_size_bytes=MAX_ATTACHMENT_SIZE_BYTES,
457	        )
458	    except ValidationError as exc:
459	        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_422_UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY, detail=str(exc))
460	
461	    # Determine file extension from original name for readability
462	    suffix = Path(original_filename).suffix.lower() or ""
463	    stored_name = f"{uuid.uuid4().hex}{suffix}"
464	
465	    dest_dir = _item_dir(item_id)
466	    dest_path = dest_dir / stored_name
467	
468	    # Write to disk
469	    dest_path.write_bytes(data)
470	
471	    # Persist metadata
472	    att = CatalogAttachment(
473	        catalog_item_id=item_id,
474	        original_filename=original_filename,
475	        stored_filename=stored_name,
476	        mime_type=declared_mime.split(";")[0].strip().lower(),
477	        file_size=fingerprint.size_bytes,
478	        sha256_fingerprint=fingerprint.sha256,
479	        uploaded_by=current_user.id,
480	    )
481	    db.add(att)
482	    db.commit()
483	    db.refresh(att)
484	    return att
485	
486	
487	@router.get("/{item_id}/attachments", response_model=list[CatalogAttachmentResponse])
488	def list_attachments(
489	    item_id: int,
490	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
491	    _: User = Depends(get_current_user),
492	):
493	    """List all attachments for a catalog item."""
494	    _get_item_or_404(db, item_id)   # confirm item exists
495	    atts = db.execute(
496	        select(CatalogAttachment)
497	        .where(CatalogAttachment.catalog_item_id == item_id)
498	        .order_by(CatalogAttachment.created_at)
499	    ).scalars().all()
500	    return atts
501	
502	
503	@router.get("/{item_id}/attachments/{att_id}/download")
504	def download_attachment(
505	    item_id: int,
506	    att_id: int,
507	    request: Request,
508	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
509	    current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
510	):
511	    """
512	    Stream an attachment file directly from the local filesystem.
513	    Performs a SHA-256 integrity check before serving; aborts if the file
514	    has been tampered with since upload.
515	    """
516	    from app.core.audit import audit
517	    from app.core.file_utils import verify_file_integrity
518	    from app.models.audit import AuditEventType
519	
520	    att = _get_attachment_or_404(db, item_id, att_id)
521	    file_path = settings.attachments_path / str(item_id) / att.stored_filename
522	
523	    if not file_path.exists():
524	        raise HTTPException(
525	            status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND,
526	            detail=(
527	                f"Attachment file not found on disk. "
528	                f"The record exists (id={att_id}) but the file is missing."
529	            ),
530	        )
531	
532	    # Integrity check before serving
533	    raw = file_path.read_bytes()
534	    if not verify_file_integrity(raw, att.sha256_fingerprint):
535	        audit(db, AuditEventType.file_integrity_fail,
536	              user_id=current_user.id, username=current_user.username,
537	              ip=request.client.host if request.client else None,
538	              resource_type="catalog_attachment", resource_id=str(att_id),
539	              detail=f"SHA-256 mismatch for {att.original_filename}")
540	        raise HTTPException(
541	            status_code=status.HTTP_409_CONFLICT,
542	            detail="File integrity check failed. The attachment may have been modified.",
543	        )
544	
545	    audit(db, AuditEventType.file_download,
546	          user_id=current_user.id, username=current_user.username,
547	          ip=request.client.host if request.client else None,
548	          resource_type="catalog_attachment", resource_id=str(att_id),
549	          detail=att.original_filename)
550	
551	    return FileResponse(
552	        path=str(file_path),
553	        media_type=att.mime_type,
554	        filename=att.original_filename,
555	    )
556	
557	
558	@router.get(
559	    "/{item_id}/attachments/{att_id}/verify",
560	    response_model=AttachmentVerifyResponse,
561	)
562	def verify_attachment(
563	    item_id: int,
564	    att_id: int,
565	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
566	    _: User = Depends(require_role(*_WRITERS)),
567	):
568	    """
569	    Offline integrity check: re-compute the file's SHA-256 and compare it
570	    to the fingerprint stored at upload time.
571	
572	    Returns integrity_ok=True only if the file exists on disk and the
573	    fingerprints match byte-for-byte.  No network access required.
574	    """
575	    att = _get_attachment_or_404(db, item_id, att_id)
576	    file_path = settings.attachments_path / str(item_id) / att.stored_filename
577	
578	    if not file_path.exists():
579	        return AttachmentVerifyResponse(
580	            attachment_id=att_id,
581	            original_filename=att.original_filename,
582	            stored_fingerprint=att.sha256_fingerprint,
583	            computed_fingerprint="",
584	            integrity_ok=False,
585	            message="File is missing from disk — it may have been deleted or moved.",
586	        )
587	
588	    data = file_path.read_bytes()
589	    ok = verify_file_integrity(data, att.sha256_fingerprint)
590	    import hashlib
591	    computed = hashlib.sha256(data).hexdigest()
592	
593	    return AttachmentVerifyResponse(
594	        attachment_id=att_id,
595	        original_filename=att.original_filename,
596	        stored_fingerprint=att.sha256_fingerprint,
597	        computed_fingerprint=computed,
598	        integrity_ok=ok,
599	        message="Integrity check passed." if ok else (
600	            "INTEGRITY FAILURE: file content has changed since upload. "
601	            "The file may have been tampered with or corrupted."
602	        ),
603	    )
604	
605	
606	@router.delete(
607	    "/{item_id}/attachments/{att_id}",
608	    status_code=status.HTTP_204_NO_CONTENT,
609	)
610	def delete_attachment(
611	    item_id: int,
612	    att_id: int,
613	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
614	    _: User = Depends(require_role(*_WRITERS)),
615	):
616	    """
617	    Delete an attachment: removes the file from disk and the database record.
618	    If the file is missing from disk the database record is still removed.
619	    """
620	    att = _get_attachment_or_404(db, item_id, att_id)
621	    file_path = settings.attachments_path / str(item_id) / att.stored_filename
622	
623	    if file_path.exists():
624	        file_path.unlink(missing_ok=True)
625	
626	    db.delete(att)
627	    db.commit()
628	
629	
630	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
631	# Allowed MIME types — informational endpoint
632	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
633	
634	@router.get("/meta/allowed-mime-types", response_model=list[str])
635	def get_allowed_mime_types(_: User = Depends(get_current_user)):
636	    """Return the list of accepted MIME types for catalog attachments."""
637	    return sorted(ALLOWED_MIME_TYPES)

9	def _pkg_payload(name: str | None = None, items: list | None = None) -> dict:
10	    if name is None:
11	        name = f"pkg_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}"
12	    payload = {
13	        "name": name,
14	        "description": "Test package",
15	        "price": "99.99",

23	def _get_or_create_exam_item(client: httpx.Client, admin_headers: dict) -> int:
24	    """Return the id of an exam item usable in package tests."""
25	    # Try fetching existing items first
26	    res = client.get("/api/exam-items", params={"limit": 1}, headers=admin_headers)
27	    if res.status_code == 200 and res.json():
28	        return res.json()[0]["id"]
29	    # Create a minimal exam item
30	    uid = uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]
31	    resp = client.post("/api/exam-items", json={
32	        "code": f"TST{uid}",
33	        "name": f"Test Item {uid}",
34	        "applicable_sex": "all",
35	    }, headers=admin_headers)
36	    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Could not create exam item: {resp.text}"
37	    return resp.json()["id"]
38	
39	
40	class TestPackageCRUD:
41	    def test_admin_can_create_package(self, client: httpx.Client, admin_headers: dict):
42	        item_id = _get_or_create_exam_item(client, admin_headers)
43	        payload = _pkg_payload(items=[{"exam_item_id": item_id, "is_required": True}])
44	        resp = client.post("/api/packages", json=payload, headers=admin_headers)
45	        assert resp.status_code == 201
46	        body = resp.json()
47	        assert body["name"] == payload["name"]
48	        assert body["version"] == 1
49	        # Cleanup
50	        client.delete(f"/api/packages/{body['id']}", headers=admin_headers)
51	
52	    def test_package_items_included_in_payload(self, client: httpx.Client, admin_headers: dict):
53	        """items array with is_required flag must be accepted by backend."""
54	        item_id = _get_or_create_exam_item(client, admin_headers)
55	        payload = _pkg_payload(items=[
56	            {"exam_item_id": item_id, "is_required": True},
57	        ])
58	        resp = client.post("/api/packages", json=payload, headers=admin_headers)
59	        assert resp.status_code == 201
60	        body = resp.json()
61	        assert len(body.get("items", [])) == 1
62	        assert body["items"][0]["exam_item_id"] == item_id
63	        assert body["items"][0]["is_required"] is True
64	        client.delete(f"/api/packages/{body['id']}", headers=admin_headers)
65

174	    ):

1	"""
2	Reviews & Moderation Module
3	============================
4	Handles review submission, image uploads, follow-ups, credibility scoring,
5	and moderator actions (pin / collapse / delete).  All data persisted offline.
6	
7	Submission rules (enforced here)
8	---------------------------------
9	1. The reviewer must be the customer who placed the order.
10	2. Anti-spam: only 1 review per order within a 10-minute window.
11	3. Follow-ups: one follow-up per initial review, within 14 days.
12	4. Images: max 6 per review, image MIME types only, max 5 MB each.
13	
14	Credibility weights
15	-------------------
16	  Verified order (completed):     base 1.0
17	  Unverified order (not completed): base 0.7
18	  Follow-up multiplier:           × 0.5
19	  Rapid-repeat penalty (<10 min): − 0.2
20	  Clamped to [0.0, 1.0]
21	
22	Moderation (admin / catalog_manager)
23	--------------------------------------
24	  pin / unpin   — surfaces review at top of per-store listings
25	  collapse / uncollapse — soft-hides body from public view
26	  delete        — admin only, hard delete
27	
28	RBAC
29	----
30	  Submit, follow-up, upload images: authenticated end_user (own orders only)
31	  Moderate: admin, catalog_manager
32	  Read: all authenticated
33	"""
34	from __future__ import annotations
35	
36	import json
37	import uuid
38	from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
39	from pathlib import Path
40

42	    APIRouter, Depends, File, HTTPException, Query,

44	)

46	from sqlalchemy import func, select
47	from sqlalchemy.orm import Session, selectinload
48

50	from app.core.dependencies import get_current_user, require_role
51	from app.core.file_utils import ValidationError, validate_upload
52	from app.core.review_credibility import recompute_and_save
53	from app.database import get_db
54	from app.models.order import Order, OrderStatus
55	from app.models.review import (
56	    MAX_REVIEW_IMAGES,
57	    REVIEW_RATE_LIMIT_MINUTES,
58	    FOLLOWUP_WINDOW_DAYS,
59	    Review,
60	    ReviewImage,
61	    ReviewSubjectType,
62	)
63	from app.models.user import User, UserRole
64	from app.schemas.review import (
65	    ModerationCollapseRequest,
66	    ModerationPinRequest,
67	    ReviewCreate,
68	    ReviewFollowupCreate,
69	    ReviewImageResponse,
70	    ReviewResponse,
71	    ReviewSortDir,
72	    ReviewSortField,
73	    ReviewSummary,
74	)
75	
76	router = APIRouter(prefix="/reviews", tags=["reviews"])
77	
78	_MODERATORS = ("admin", "catalog_manager")
79	
80	# Only image MIME types are accepted for review images

1	from cryptography.fernet import Fernet
2	from app.config import settings
3	
4	
5	class FieldEncryptor:
6	    def __init__(self, key: str):
7	        # Ensure proper padding for base64 key
8	        key_bytes = key.encode() if isinstance(key, str) else key
9	        self._fernet = Fernet(key_bytes)
10	
11	    def encrypt(self, text: str) -> str:
12	        if not text:
13	            return text
14	        return self._fernet.encrypt(text.encode()).decode()
15	
16	    def decrypt(self, ciphertext: str) -> str:
17	        if not ciphertext:
18	            return ciphertext
19	        return self._fernet.decrypt(ciphertext.encode()).decode()
20	
21	
22	encryptor = FieldEncryptor(settings.ENCRYPTION_KEY)
23

19	@router.post("", response_model=UserResponse, status_code=status.HTTP_201_CREATED)
20	def create_user(
21	    payload: UserCreate,
22	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
23	    _: User = Depends(require_role("admin")),
24	):
25	    existing = db.execute(
26	        select(User).where(
27	            (User.username == payload.username) | (User.email == payload.email)
28	        )
29	    ).scalar_one_or_none()
30	    if existing:
31	        raise HTTPException(
32	            status_code=status.HTTP_409_CONFLICT,
33	            detail="Username or email already in use",
34	        )
35	    user = User(
36	        username=payload.username,
37	        email=payload.email,
38	        hashed_password=hash_password(payload.password),
39	        role=payload.role,
40	    )
41	    db.add(user)
42	    db.commit()
43	    db.refresh(user)
44	    return user
45	
46	
47	@router.get("", response_model=list[UserResponse])
48	def list_users(
49	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
50	    _: User = Depends(require_role("admin")),
51	    skip: int = 0,
52	    limit: int = 100,
53	):
54	    users = db.execute(select(User).offset(skip).limit(limit)).scalars().all()
55	    return users

1	from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException, Request, status
2	from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
3	from sqlalchemy.orm import Session
4	from sqlalchemy import select
5	from app.database import get_db
6	from app.models.user import User
7	from app.core.security import hash_password, verify_password
8	from app.core.dependencies import get_current_user, require_role
9	from app.schemas.user import UserCreate, UserUpdate, UserResponse
10	
11	
12	class PasswordChangeRequest(BaseModel):
13	    current_password: str = Field(min_length=1)
14	    new_password: str = Field(min_length=10, description="Min 10 characters")
15	
16	router = APIRouter(prefix="/users", tags=["users"])
17	
18	
19	@router.post("", response_model=UserResponse, status_code=status.HTTP_201_CREATED)
20	def create_user(
21	    payload: UserCreate,
22	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
23	    _: User = Depends(require_role("admin")),
24	):
25	    existing = db.execute(
26	        select(User).where(
27	            (User.username == payload.username) | (User.email == payload.email)
28	        )
29	    ).scalar_one_or_none()
30	    if existing:
31	        raise HTTPException(
32	            status_code=status.HTTP_409_CONFLICT,
33	            detail="Username or email already in use",
34	        )
35	    user = User(
36	        username=payload.username,
37	        email=payload.email,
38	        hashed_password=hash_password(payload.password),
39	        role=payload.role,
40	    )
41	    db.add(user)
42	    db.commit()
43	    db.refresh(user)
44	    return user
45	
46	
47	@router.get("", response_model=list[UserResponse])
48	def list_users(
49	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
50	    _: User = Depends(require_role("admin")),
51	    skip: int = 0,
52	    limit: int = 100,
53	):
54	    users = db.execute(select(User).offset(skip).limit(limit)).scalars().all()
55	    return users
56	
57	
58	@router.get("/me", response_model=UserResponse)
59	def get_me(current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user)):
60	    return current_user
61	
62	
63	@router.get("/{user_id}", response_model=UserResponse)
64	def get_user(
65	    user_id: int,
66	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
67	    _: User = Depends(require_role("admin")),
68	):
69	    user = db.execute(select(User).where(User.id == user_id)).scalar_one_or_none()
70	    if not user:
71	        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, detail="User not found")
72	    return user
73	
74	
75	@router.put("/me", response_model=UserResponse)
76	def update_me(
77	    payload: UserUpdate,
78	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
79	    current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
80	):
81	    if payload.email is not None:
82	        current_user.email = payload.email
83	    if payload.password is not None:
84	        current_user.hashed_password = hash_password(payload.password)
85	    # Non-admin cannot change their own role or active status
86	    db.commit()
87	    db.refresh(current_user)
88	    return current_user
89	
90	
91	@router.put("/{user_id}", response_model=UserResponse)
92	def update_user(
93	    user_id: int,
94	    payload: UserUpdate,
95	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
96	    _: User = Depends(require_role("admin")),
97	):
98	    user = db.execute(select(User).where(User.id == user_id)).scalar_one_or_none()
99	    if not user:
100	        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, detail="User not found")
101	    if payload.email is not None:
102	        user.email = payload.email
103	    if payload.password is not None:
104	        user.hashed_password = hash_password(payload.password)
105	    if payload.role is not None:
106	        user.role = payload.role
107	    if payload.is_active is not None:
108	        user.is_active = payload.is_active
109	    db.commit()
110	    db.refresh(user)
111	    return user
112	
113	
114	@router.post("/me/change-password", status_code=status.HTTP_204_NO_CONTENT)
115	def change_password(
116	    payload: PasswordChangeRequest,
117	    request: Request,
118	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
119	    current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
120	):
121	    """Change the authenticated user's own password.  Audited; invalidates all sessions."""
122	    from app.core.audit import audit
123	    from app.models.audit import AuditEventType
124	
125	    if not verify_password(payload.current_password, current_user.hashed_password):
126	        audit(db, AuditEventType.login_failure,
127	              user_id=current_user.id, username=current_user.username,
128	              ip=request.client.host if request.client else None,
129	              detail="wrong current_password on change-password")
130	        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED,
131	                            detail="Current password is incorrect")
132	
133	    current_user.hashed_password = hash_password(payload.new_password)
134	    # Rotate session hash → forces re-login on all devices
135	    current_user.session_token_hash = None
136	    db.commit()
137	
138	    audit(db, AuditEventType.password_change,
139	          user_id=current_user.id, username=current_user.username,
140	          ip=request.client.host if request.client else None,
141	          detail="password changed; all sessions invalidated")
142	
143	
144	@router.delete("/{user_id}", status_code=status.HTTP_204_NO_CONTENT)
145	def delete_user(
146	    user_id: int,
147	    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
148	    current_user: User = Depends(require_role("admin")),
149	):
150	    if user_id == current_user.id:
151	        raise HTTPException(
152	            status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
153	            detail="Cannot delete your own account",
154	        )
155	    user = db.execute(select(User).where(User.id == user_id)).scalar_one_or_none()
156	    if not user:
157	        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, detail="User not found")
158	    db.delete(user)
159	    db.commit()
160

1	"""TraceCare Offline Compliance Portal — FastAPI application entry point.
2	
3	Security features wired here:
4	  • OfflineSecurityMiddleware — private-IP enforcement + hardened response headers
5	  • SensitiveDataFilter      — token / password redaction from all log sinks
6	  • CORS restricted to localhost origins only (no external access)
7	"""
8	import logging
9	
10	from fastapi import FastAPI
11	from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
12	
13	from app.config import settings
14	from app.core.log_filter import configure_secure_logging
15	from app.core.security_middleware import OfflineSecurityMiddleware
16	from app.routers import (
17	    admin, audit, auth, catalog, cms, exam_items, exams,
18	    messages, notifications, packages, products, reviews, users,
19	)
20	
21	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
22	# Logging — install redaction filter before anything else logs
23	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
24	configure_secure_logging()
25	
26	logger = logging.getLogger("tracecare")
27	
28	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
29	# Application
30	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
31	app = FastAPI(
32	    title="TraceCare Offline Compliance Portal",
33	    description="Fully offline/on-prem compliance management system",
34	    version="1.0.0",
35	    # Disable OpenAPI in production if desired:
36	    # openapi_url=None,
37	)
38	
39	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
40	# Security middleware (runs before CORS)
41	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
42	app.add_middleware(
43	    OfflineSecurityMiddleware,
44	    enforce_local_only=True,  # Set False only for dev/test against public IP
45	)
46	
47	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
48	# CORS — localhost only (belt-and-suspenders alongside IP enforcement)
49	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
50	app.add_middleware(
51	    CORSMiddleware,
52	    allow_origins=[
53	        "http://localhost:3000",
54	        "http://localhost:5173",
55	        "http://127.0.0.1:3000",
56	        "http://127.0.0.1:5173",
57	    ],
58	    allow_credentials=True,
59	    allow_methods=["*"],
60	    allow_headers=["*"],
61	)
62	
63	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
64	# Routers
65	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
66	_API = "/api"
67	app.include_router(auth.router,          prefix=_API)
68	app.include_router(users.router,         prefix=_API)
69	app.include_router(exam_items.router,    prefix=_API)
70	app.include_router(packages.router,      prefix=_API)
71	app.include_router(exams.router,         prefix=_API)
72	app.include_router(products.router,      prefix=_API)
73	app.include_router(catalog.router,       prefix=_API)
74	app.include_router(messages.router,      prefix=_API)
75	app.include_router(notifications.router, prefix=_API)

    def test_diff_between_versions(self, client: httpx.Client, admin_headers: dict):
        item_id = _get_or_create_exam_item(client, admin_headers)
        pkg_v1 = client.post("/api/packages", json=_pkg_payload(
            items=[{"exam_item_id": item_id, "is_required": True}]
        ), headers=admin_headers).json()
        pkg_id = pkg_v1["id"]

        pkg_v2 = client.post(f"/api/packages/{pkg_id}/new-version", json={
            "description": "New description",
            "price": "199.99",
            "validity_window_days": 90,
        }, headers=admin_headers).json()

        # Diff endpoint: GET /packages/{base_id}/diff/{other_id}
        resp = client.get(
            f"/api/packages/{pkg_id}/diff/{pkg_v2['id']}",
            headers=admin_headers,
        )
        assert resp.status_code == 200, f"diff failed: {resp.text}"
        diff = resp.json()
        assert "metadata_changes" in diff
        assert "items_added" in diff
        assert "items_removed" in diff
        assert "items_changed" in diff
        # Verify metadata_changes use from_value/to_value (not before/after)
        for change in diff.get("metadata_changes", []):
            assert "from_value" in change, "metadata change must use 'from_value'"
            assert "to_value" in change, "metadata change must use 'to_value'"

        client.delete(f"/api/packages/{pkg_id}", headers=admin_headers)
        client.delete(f"/api/packages/{pkg_v2['id']}", headers=admin_headers)

    def test_diff_same_package_returns_422(self, client: httpx.Client, admin_headers: dict):
        item_id = _get_or_create_exam_item(client, admin_headers)
        pkg = client.post("/api/packages", json=_pkg_payload(
            items=[{"exam_item_id": item_id, "is_required": True}]
        ), headers=admin_headers).json()
        resp = client.get(f"/api/packages/{pkg['id']}/diff/{pkg['id']}", headers=admin_headers)
        assert resp.status_code == 422
        client.delete(f"/api/packages/{pkg['id']}", headers=admin_headers)"}

        assert resp.status_code == 403

    def test_search_filter_works(self, client: httpx.Client, admin_headers: dict):
        """Full-text search across name/description/category/origin."""
        unique_term = f"xqz{uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}"
        payload = _catalog_payload(name=f"Item {unique_term}")
        item = client.post("/api/catalog", json=payload, headers=admin_headers).json()
        resp = client.get("/api/catalog", params={"search": unique_term, "active_only": False},
                          headers=admin_headers)
        assert resp.status_code == 200
        ids = [r["id"] for r in resp.json()]
        assert item["id"] in ids
        client.delete(f"/api/catalog/{item['id']}", headers=admin_headers)

    def test_price_range_filter(self, client: httpx.Client, admin_headers: dict):
        payload = _catalog_payload()
        payload["price"] = "250.00"
        item = client.post("/api/catalog", json=payload, headers=admin_headers).json()
        # Should appear in range 200-300
        resp = client.get("/api/catalog", params={"price_min": 200, "price_max": 300,
                          "active_only": False}, headers=admin_headers)
        assert resp.status_code == 200
        ids = [r["id"] for r in resp.json()]
        assert item["id"] in ids
        # Should NOT appear in range 0-100
        resp2 = client.get("/api/catalog", params={"price_min": 0, "price_max": 100,
                           "active_only": False}, headers=admin_headers)
        ids2 = [r["id"] for r in resp2.json()]
        assert item["id"] not in ids2
        client.delete(f"/api/catalog/{item['id']}", headers=admin_headers)"}

    def test_unauthenticated_download_rejected(self, client: httpx.Client, admin_headers: dict):
        """Files must NOT be reachable by unauthenticated requests."""
        item = client.post("/api/catalog", json=_catalog_payload(), headers=admin_headers).json()
        item_id = item["id"]
        attach = client.post(
            f"/api/catalog/{item_id}/attachments",
            files={"file": ("doc.pdf", io.BytesIO(_pdf_bytes()), "application/pdf")},
            headers=admin_headers,
        ).json()
        attach_id = attach["id"]

        # No auth headers — must be rejected
        resp = client.get(f"/api/catalog/{item_id}/attachments/{attach_id}/download")
        assert resp.status_code in (401, 403), (
            f"Unauthenticated download should be rejected, got {resp.status_code}"
        )

        # Direct /uploads path must not exist (no static mount)
        static_resp = client.get(f"/uploads/catalog/{item_id}/{attach.get('stored_filename', 'x')}")
        assert static_resp.status_code in (401, 403, 404), (
            f"/uploads must not serve files unauthenticated, got {static_resp.status_code}"
        )

        client.delete(f"/api/catalog/{item_id}", headers=admin_headers)

    def test_other_user_cannot_download_via_object_id(
        self, client: httpx.Client, admin_headers: dict, temp_end_user_headers: dict
    ):
        """End users without catalog_manager role cannot download attachments."""
        item = client.post("/api/catalog", json=_catalog_payload(), headers=admin_headers).json()
        item_id = item["id"]
        attach = client.post(
            f"/api/catalog/{item_id}/attachments",
            files={"file": ("doc.pdf", io.BytesIO(_pdf_bytes()), "application/pdf")},
            headers=admin_headers,
        ).json()
        attach_id = attach["id"]

        # End user CAN download (read is allowed for all authenticated)
        resp = client.get(
            f"/api/catalog/{item_id}/attachments/{attach_id}/download",
            headers=temp_end_user_headers,
        )
        assert resp.status_code == 200

        # End user CANNOT delete attachment
        del_resp = client.delete(
            f"/api/catalog/{item_id}/attachments/{attach_id}",
            headers=temp_end_user_headers,
        )
        assert del_resp.status_code == 403

        client.delete(f"/api/catalog/{item_id}", headers=admin_headers)"}
